name: Prone Swimmers
description: Alternating arm/leg extension for posterior chain.
instructions: Prone, alternately lift opposite arm and leg.
clinical_notes: Gentle posterior chain exercise.

name: Wall Sit with Calf Raise
description: Isometric quad combined with active calf.
instructions: Hold wall sit position at 90 deg and perform calf raises.
clinical_notes: Increases lower leg demand during isometric knee hold.

name: Hip Flexor Mobilisation
description: Active hip flexor mobility in half-kneeling.
instructions: Start in half-kneeling, gently shift weight forward to stretch front of hip. Maintain upright posture.
clinical_notes: Ensure anterior pelvic tilt is avoided. Useful for hip flexor tightness.

name: Hip Capsule Mobilisation (Posterior)
description: Joint mobilisation for posterior hip capsule restriction.
instructions: In quadruped, shift weight backward keeping knee at 90°. Apply gentle oscillatory pressure into hip flexion.
clinical_notes: Grade III-IV mobilisation.

name: Hip 90/90 Stretch
description: Combined hip IR/ER mobility drill.
instructions: Sit with both knees at 90°, one forward and one to side. Lean torso over front leg.
clinical_notes: Excellent for hip rotational mobility.

name: Knee-to-Wall Ankle Dorsiflexion
description: Weight-bearing ankle dorsiflexion mobility drill.
instructions: Stand facing wall, push knee toward wall keeping heel flat.
clinical_notes: Gold standard ankle DF assessment and treatment.

name: Pigeon Pose Stretch
description: Hip external rotator and capsule mobilisation.
instructions: From quadruped, bring front knee to floor at angle, extend back leg, lower chest toward floor.
clinical_notes: Modify for patients with knee pain.

name: Figure-4 Glute Stretch (Supine)
description: Supine piriformis and external rotator stretch.
instructions: Lie on back, cross ankle over opposite knee, pull both legs toward chest.
clinical_notes: Excellent alternative to pigeon pose.

name: Hamstring Stretch (Standing)
description: Active hamstring flexibility stretch.
instructions: Place heel on elevated surface, hinge at hip keeping spine neutral. Feel stretch in posterior thigh.
clinical_notes: Maintain neutral lumbar spine — avoid rounding back.

name: Standing Quad Stretch
description: Rectus femoris and quad flexibility.
instructions: Stand on one leg, pull opposite heel to buttocks. Hold ankle, keep knees together.
clinical_notes: Useful for patellofemoral pain and knee rehabilitation.

name: ITB Stretch (Standing)
description: Iliotibial band and TFL stretch.
instructions: Cross affected leg behind, reach opposite arm overhead and lean away. Feel lateral hip stretch.
clinical_notes: For ITBS and hip abductor tightness. Combine with strengthening.

name: Calf Stretch (Wall)
description: Gastrocnemius and soleus stretching.
instructions: Place hands on wall, step back, keep heel flat. Bend knee for soleus, keep straight for gastrocnemius.
sets_reps: 3 x 30 sec each position each side
clinical_notes: Essential for plantar fasciitis and Achilles tendinopathy.

name: Clamshell Exercise
description: Hip ER strengthening for gluteus medius.
instructions: Lie on side, hips/knees bent 45°/90°. Open top knee like clamshell keeping feet together.
clinical_notes: Fundamental hip stabilization exercise. Progress with resistance band.

name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
description: Eccentric hamstring strengthening for injury prevention.
instructions: Kneel with feet anchored, lower torso toward floor under control, catch with hands. Curl back up.
clinical_notes: Reduces hamstring injury risk by 51%. Eccentric loading at long muscle length.

name: Prone Hamstring Curl
description: Prone resisted hamstring curl.
instructions: Lie prone, attach resistance at ankle. Curl heel toward buttocks through full range.
clinical_notes: Standard hamstring isolation exercise.

name: Single Leg Romanian Deadlift
description: Unilateral hip hinge for posterior chain and balance.
instructions: Stand on one leg, hinge forward at hip while extending free leg behind. Keep spine neutral.
clinical_notes: Key exercise in ACL rehab and hip stability.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
description: Rear-foot elevated split squat for unilateral quad and glute.
instructions: Place rear foot on bench, lower front knee toward floor keeping torso upright.
clinical_notes: High demand exercise; ensure adequate hip flexor length.

name: Lateral Band Walk
description: Resisted lateral stepping for hip abductor activation.
instructions: Place resistance band around ankles/thighs. Step laterally maintaining slight knee bend.
clinical_notes: Key for PFPS and hip stabilization.

name: Hip Thrust
description: Barbell or bodyweight glute bridge with shoulder support.
instructions: Upper back on bench, drive hips up through heel push. Squeeze glutes at top.
clinical_notes: Highest glute-activating exercise. Essential in glute programs.

name: Lateral Step-Up
description: Lateral step-up for hip abductor and quad.
instructions: Stand beside step, step up laterally using the lead leg. Control descent.
clinical_notes: Progress by increasing step height or load.

name: Single Leg Press
description: Unilateral leg press for quad/glute/hamstring loading.
instructions: On leg press machine, use one leg at a time. Full ROM, controlled descent.
clinical_notes: Controlled loading for early rehab.

name: Single Leg Calf Raise
description: Soleus and gastrocnemius strengthening.
instructions: Stand on edge of step on one foot, lower heel below step, rise to toes.
clinical_notes: Essential for Achilles tendinopathy (Alfredson protocol).

name: Terminal Knee Extension (TKE)
description: VMO and quad activation near full extension.
instructions: Anchor resistance band behind knee, straighten knee against resistance.
clinical_notes: Ideal for early post-op knee rehab.

name: Glute Bridge
description: Supine glute bridge for gluteus maximus and hamstring.
instructions: Lie on back, feet flat, drive hips up squeezing glutes at top.
clinical_notes: Foundational glute exercise. Progress to single leg.

name: Copenhagen Hip Adduction
description: Eccentric adductor strengthening for groin injury prevention.
instructions: Side-lying, top foot on bench/partner, lift hips up using adductor strength. Control descent.
clinical_notes: Reduces groin injury incidence in footballers. Progress from bent to straight knee.

name: Reverse Nordic Curl
description: Eccentric quadriceps strengthening.
instructions: Kneel with feet anchored, lean back under control maintaining neutral spine. Push back up.
clinical_notes: For patellar tendinopathy and quad strengthening at long lengths.

name: Isometric Wall Squat
description: Isometric quad contraction at 90° for knee rehab.
instructions: Back against wall, slide down to 90° knee flexion. Hold contraction.
clinical_notes: Analgesic effect for patellar tendinopathy. Safe early phase quad loading.

name: Short Arc Quad (SAQ)
description: Terminal extension strengthening for VMO.
instructions: Seated with towel roll under knee at ~40°. Extend knee to full extension. Lower slowly.
clinical_notes: Low compressive load on patellofemoral joint. Early phase post-op.

name: Decline Squat (Single Leg)
description: Eccentric quad loading for patellar tendinopathy.
instructions: Stand on 25° decline board, slowly squat on one leg. Control 3-4 seconds down.
clinical_notes: Gold standard for patellar tendinopathy (eccentric protocol).

name: Hip Abduction (Side-lying)
description: Isolated hip abductor strengthening.
instructions: Side-lying, lift top leg to 30-40° keeping foot neutral. Control descent.
clinical_notes: For GTPS, PFPS, and hip stabilization programs.

name: Towel Curl (Plantar Fascia Loading)
description: High-load plantar fascia strengthening with Rathleff protocol.
instructions: Stand with towel folded under toes, perform single leg calf raise with toes extended.
clinical_notes: Rathleff protocol: superior to stretching alone at 3 months for plantar fasciitis.

name: Peroneal Strengthening (Band Eversion)
description: Peroneus longus/brevis strengthening for ankle stability.
instructions: Sit with resistance band around foot. Evert foot against resistance. Control return.
clinical_notes: Essential for lateral ankle sprain rehab and chronic instability.

name: Bosu Ball Squat
description: Unstable surface squat for proprioception.
instructions: Stand on dome side of BOSU, perform squat maintaining balance.
clinical_notes: Increases neuromuscular demand. Use only when adequate strength established.

name: Single Leg Balance (Eyes Closed)
description: Enhanced proprioceptive challenge without visual input.
instructions: Stand on one leg, close eyes, maintain balance.
clinical_notes: Essential after ankle sprains and for chronic instability.

name: Star Excursion Balance
description: Multi-directional reach training on single leg.
instructions: Stand on one leg, reach opposite leg in anterior, posteromedial, and posterolateral directions.
clinical_notes: Both assessment and training tool.

name: Wobble Board Balance
description: Multi-axial ankle proprioception training.
instructions: Stand on wobble board, maintain balance first double then single leg.
clinical_notes: Reduces ankle re-injury risk by 35%. Essential in lateral ankle sprain rehab.

name: Box Jump
description: Plyometric jump onto box for power.
instructions: Countermovement jump landing softly on box.
clinical_notes: Landing mechanics are key before progression.

name: Drop Jump
description: Depth landing for eccentric loading and reactive power.
instructions: Step off box, land and immediately jump vertically. Minimize ground contact time.
clinical_notes: High-intensity reactive exercise for advanced rehab.

name: Lateral Hop Test/Training
description: Lateral plyometric for ankle/knee stability and power.
instructions: Hop laterally over a line on single leg. Land softly, control balance.
clinical_notes: Return-to-sport test and training. Criteria: LSI >90%.

name: Serratus Anterior Punch
description: Serratus anterior strengthening in supine.
instructions: Lie supine, arm pointing to ceiling, push arm further toward ceiling rounding scapula.
clinical_notes: Key for scapular winging and shoulder impingement.

name: Push-Up Plus
description: Push-up with scapular protraction for serratus activation.
instructions: Perform push-up, add extra push to protract scapula at top.
clinical_notes: Highest serratus activation in weight-bearing.

name: Wall Slide
description: Scapular upward rotation against wall.
instructions: Arms against wall in W position, slide to Y overhead maintaining contact.
clinical_notes: Progresses scapular upward rotation pattern.

name: Prone T (Middle Trapezius)
description: Prone T-exercise for middle and lower trapezius.
instructions: Lie prone, arms in T position, lift arms toward ceiling squeezing scapulae together.
clinical_notes: Targets often-weak mid-trapezius.

name: Prone Y (Lower Trapezius)
description: Prone Y-exercise for lower trapezius and scapular depression.
instructions: Lie prone, arms in Y position overhead, lift arms controlling scapular depression.
clinical_notes: Lower trapezius is commonly inhibited in shoulder pathology.

name: Side-Lying External Rotation
description: Shoulder ER for rotator cuff (infraspinatus/teres minor).
instructions: Lie on side, elbow at 90°, rotate forearm toward ceiling. Control descent.
clinical_notes: Foundational rotator cuff exercise.

name: Isometric Shoulder ER
description: Isometric external rotation for rotator cuff.
instructions: Press back of hand into wall with elbow at 90°. Hold contraction.
clinical_notes: Safe early-phase exercise with analgesic effect.

name: Tyler Twist (Eccentric Wrist Extension)
description: Eccentric wrist extension for lateral epicondylitis.
instructions: Hold bar with both hands, extend with unaffected hand, lower (eccentric) with affected.
clinical_notes: Gold standard for lateral epicondylitis.

name: Shoulder IR (Side-lying)
description: Shoulder internal rotation for subscapularis.
instructions: Lie on affected side, elbow at 90°, rotate forearm down toward abdomen. Control return.
clinical_notes: Targets subscapularis. Part of rotator cuff balanced program.

name: Rotator Cuff ER at 90° ABD
description: High-demand ER strengthening in abduction for overhead athletes.
instructions: Elbow at shoulder height, 90° flexion. Rotate forearm toward ceiling. Control return.
clinical_notes: For overhead athletes and rotator cuff rehab phase 2-3.

name: Prone Shoulder Abduction (W Exercise)
description: Scapular stabilizer strengthening in W position.
instructions: Lie prone with arms in W shape, lift arms squeezing scapulae down and together.
clinical_notes: Addresses periscapular weakness common in shoulder impingement.

name: Band Shoulder Diagonal (PNF D2)
description: Functional diagonal pattern for shoulder complex.
instructions: D2 flexion: start low-cross-body, sweep diagonally up-out to ER. D2 extension: reverse.
clinical_notes: PNF patterns integrate rotator cuff with functional movement.

name: Elbow Flexion Curl (Resisted)
description: Biceps strengthening for elbow flexion.
instructions: Standing or seated, curl weight from hip to shoulder with supinated forearm. Control descent.
clinical_notes: Early phase elbow rehab. Progress to eccentric loading.

name: Triceps Push-Down
description: Triceps strengthening with cable/band.
instructions: Stand at cable/band, push handle down extending elbow. Control return.
clinical_notes: Posterior elbow strengthening and tennis elbow programs.

name: Wrist Flexion Curl (Resisted)
description: Wrist flexor strengthening for medial epicondylitis.
instructions: Forearm on table, palm up. Curl wrist up against resistance. Lower slowly.
clinical_notes: For medial epicondylitis (golfer's elbow) progressive loading.

name: Forearm Supination/Pronation
description: Forearm rotator strengthening for elbow rehab.
instructions: Hold hammer or band, rotate forearm from pronation to supination. Control through range.
clinical_notes: For UCL and lateral epicondylitis rehab.

name: Grip Strengthening (Hand Squeeze)
description: Grip force and forearm flexor strengthening.
instructions: Squeeze putty/stress ball/grip device with full force. Hold 5 sec, release.
clinical_notes: For CTS, trigger finger, and general hand rehabilitation.

name: Shoulder Diagonal Band Press (Punch)
description: Functional rotator cuff and scapular stabilizer activation.
instructions: Anchor band behind body, punch diagonally forward-upward. Control return.
clinical_notes: Sport-specific for overhead athletes.

name: Sleeper Stretch (Posterior Capsule)
description: Posterior shoulder capsule stretch for GIRD.
instructions: Side-lying on affected arm (90° shoulder, 90° elbow), use other arm to push wrist toward floor.
clinical_notes: For GIRD and shoulder impingement. Avoid if painful.

name: Cross-Body Shoulder Stretch
description: Posterior shoulder and capsule flexibility.
instructions: Pull arm across chest with opposite hand. Hold stretch.
clinical_notes: Common stretch for posterior shoulder tightness.

name: Wrist Extensor Stretch
description: Wrist extensor flexibility for lateral epicondylitis.
instructions: Extend arm, use other hand to gently flex wrist. Hold stretch.
clinical_notes: Essential stretching component for tennis elbow management.

name: Dead Bug
description: Core stability with reciprocal limb movement.
instructions: Lie supine, arms ceiling, hips/knees 90°. Lower opposite arm/leg maintaining lumbar neutral.
clinical_notes: Anti-extension core exercise. Ideal for LBP.

name: Bird Dog
description: Core and spinal stability with reciprocal limb extension.
instructions: In quadruped, extend opposite arm and leg maintaining neutral spine.
clinical_notes: McGill's Big 3. Essential for LBP rehabilitation.

name: Pallof Press
description: Anti-rotation core stability with band or cable.
instructions: Stand sideways to anchor, press band directly in front maintaining neutral spine.
clinical_notes: Anti-rotation exercise targeting obliques.

name: Side Plank
description: Lateral core stability exercise.
instructions: Support body on forearm and feet, maintain straight line.
clinical_notes: McGill's Big 3. Targets QL and lateral stabilizers.

name: Plank
description: Prone plank for core endurance.
instructions: Support on forearms and toes, maintain neutral spine, breathe normally.
clinical_notes: McGill's Big 3 variation. Avoid breath holding.

name: McGill Curl-Up
description: Spine-sparing abdominal curl.
instructions: One knee bent, hands under lumbar spine, small curl lifting head and shoulders only.
clinical_notes: McGill's Big 3. Maintains lumbar curve.

name: Copenhagen Plank
description: Adductor and lateral core strengthening.
instructions: Side-lying, top foot on bench. Lift hips maintaining straight body. Hold.
clinical_notes: For groin injury prevention and lateral core strength.

name: Hollow Body Hold
description: Anterior core stability in supine.
instructions: Supine, extend arms overhead, press lower back into floor, raise legs/shoulders. Hold.
clinical_notes: Gymnastics-based exercise for anterior core stiffness.

name: Suitcase Carry
description: Unilateral loaded carry for lateral core and hip stability.
instructions: Hold kettlebell/dumbbell in one hand, walk tall without lateral lean. Maintain neutral spine.
clinical_notes: Anti-lateral flexion exercise. Excellent for lumbar stability.

name: Cat-Cow Stretch
description: Spinal mobility alternating flexion/extension.
instructions: In quadruped, alternate arching (cat) and dropping (cow) spine.
clinical_notes: Gentle lumbar mobilisation for acute and subacute phases.

name: McKenzie Press-Up (Lumbar Extension)
description: McKenzie lumbar extension for disc centralisation.
instructions: Lie prone, press up with hands letting lumbar sag. Sustained end-range extensions.
clinical_notes: Directional preference exercise. Use when extension centralises symptoms.

name: Cervical Retraction (Chin Tuck)
description: Deep neck flexor activation and cervical posture correction.
instructions: Draw chin straight back creating 'double chin'. Hold and release.
clinical_notes: Key for cervicogenic headache and radiculopathy.

name: Thoracic Extension Over Foam Roller
description: Thoracic extension mobilisation.
instructions: Place foam roller at mid-thorax, support head with hands, extend over roller.
clinical_notes: Target T4-T8 segments.

name: Deep Neck Flexor Exercise (CCFT)
description: Cranio-cervical flexion for deep neck flexor rehabilitation.
instructions: Supine with pressure biofeedback at C4. Nod head gently (upper cervical flexion). Hold 10 sec at each increment.
sets_reps: 3 x 10 x 10 sec at each level
clinical_notes: Evidence-based for cervicogenic headache, WAD, and cervical radiculopathy.

name: Cervical Rotation Stretch
description: Active cervical rotation mobility.
instructions: Seated upright, slowly rotate head side to side through comfortable range. Hold 5 sec.
clinical_notes: For cervical stiffness, WAD, and facet joint dysfunction.

name: Cervical Lateral Flexion Stretch
description: Scalene and upper trap stretch with lateral flexion.
instructions: Tilt head to one side, apply gentle overpressure with hand. Feel stretch on opposite side.
clinical_notes: For TOS, WAD, and cervicogenic headache.

name: Suboccipital Release / Stretch
description: Suboccipital muscle lengthening for cervicogenic headache.
instructions: Lie supine, tuck chin gently to lengthen suboccipital region. Self-sustained traction optional.
clinical_notes: Highly effective for cervicogenic headache. Can use towel for gentle traction.

name: Scapular Retraction (Band Pull-Apart)
description: Mid trapezius and rhomboid strengthening.
instructions: Hold band at shoulder width, pull apart horizontally squeezing scapulae together.
clinical_notes: Postural correction and thoracic spine function.

name: Thoracic Rotation (Seated)
description: Active thoracic rotation mobility exercise.
instructions: Seated upright with arms crossed or hands behind head. Rotate trunk side to side through full range.
clinical_notes: For shoulder impingement and thoracic stiffness. Target >40° each side.

name: Prone Hip Extension (Lumbar Multifidus)
description: Isolated multifidus activation in prone.
instructions: Lie prone, contract glute and lift leg keeping knee extended. Hold 5 sec.
clinical_notes: For lumbar segmental stabilisation. Key in LBP programs.

name: Turkish Get-Up
description: Full-body functional stability exercise.
instructions: Starting supine with kettlebell overhead, perform sequence to standing and reverse.
clinical_notes: Advanced exercise. Excellent shoulder stability development.

name: World's Greatest Stretch
description: Full-body mobility for hip, thorax, and shoulder.
instructions: Lunge forward, rotate trunk open, reach arm to ceiling.
clinical_notes: Excellent warm-up drill.

name: Kettlebell Swing
description: Hip hinge power and posterior chain conditioning.
instructions: Hinge at hip, swing kettlebell between legs, drive hips forward.
clinical_notes: Hip hinge pattern must be established before loading.

name: Farmer's Carry
description: Loaded bilateral carry for full-body stability and grip.
instructions: Hold weights bilaterally, walk tall maintaining upright posture.
clinical_notes: For return to work and functional strengthening.

name: Overhead Press (Dumbbell)
description: Shoulder and scapular stabilizer overhead loading.
instructions: Start dumbbells at shoulder height, press overhead maintaining rib cage neutral.
clinical_notes: Functional overhead loading. Prerequisites: full overhead mobility, adequate rotator cuff strength.

name: Deadlift
description: Hip hinge compound lift for posterior chain.
instructions: Feet hip-width, hinge at hip, push floor away maintaining neutral spine. Lock out at top.
clinical_notes: Foundation posterior chain movement. Important for LBP rehabilitation (load tolerance).

name: Goblet Squat
description: Anterior loaded squat for quad/glute and thoracic posture.
instructions: Hold kettlebell at chest height, squat deep keeping upright torso.
clinical_notes: Anterior load promotes upright thorax. Good for beginners.

name: Half-Kneeling Press
description: Unilateral overhead press in half-kneeling for lumbar-hip stability.
instructions: Half-kneeling position, press single dumbbell overhead maintaining hip-neutral position.
clinical_notes: Integrates hip stability with shoulder loading.

name: Wall Squat (Static)
description: Isometric quad exercise at 60-90°.
instructions: Back against wall, slide down to 60-90° knee flexion. Hold.
clinical_notes: Analgesic for patellar tendinopathy.

name: Quad Set
description: Isometric quadriceps contraction in full extension.
instructions: Press knee into floor contracting quad. Hold 10 sec, release.
clinical_notes: First post-op quad activation. Zero PFJ compressive load.

name: Seated Leg Extension
description: Isolated knee extension for quadriceps.
instructions: Seated on machine, extend knee through full ROM. Control descent 3 seconds.
clinical_notes: Use 60-0° ROM for PFPS patients.

name: Forward Step-Up
description: Anterior step-up for quad and glute.
instructions: Step up onto box with lead leg, drive through heel. Control descent slowly.
clinical_notes: Used in ACL and PFPS rehab.

name: Bilateral Squat
description: Fundamental bilateral squat.
instructions: Feet shoulder-width, descend until thighs parallel. Drive up through heels.
clinical_notes: Foundation exercise. Monitor knee valgus.

name: Hack Squat (Machine)
description: Machine squat for quad-dominant loading.
instructions: Controlled descent to 90°, drive through full extension.
clinical_notes: Heavy slow resistance for patellar tendinopathy (Kongsgaard 2009).

name: Isometric Leg Press (Patellar Tendon)
description: Isometric quad at 60° for tendon pain management.
instructions: On leg press at 60°, push at 70% max force, hold 45 seconds. Do not move platform.
clinical_notes: Immediate analgesic effect — Rio et al. in-season protocol.

name: Eccentric Hamstring Curl (Single Leg)
description: Eccentric-focused hamstring curl.
instructions: Concentric with both legs, eccentric lowering single leg 4 seconds.
clinical_notes: Key for hamstring strain rehab at long muscle lengths.

name: Good Morning
description: Hinge-pattern hamstring and glute loading.
instructions: Bar on back, hinge at hip neutral spine. Feel hamstring stretch, drive hips forward.
clinical_notes: Hip hinge for posterior chain.

name: Single Leg Hamstring Bridge (Heel on Box)
description: Hamstring-dominant single leg bridge.
instructions: Heel on box, single leg bridge driving through heel. Control descent.
clinical_notes: Heel placement increases hamstring demand.

name: Stiff Leg Deadlift
description: Hamstring loading through hip hinge.
instructions: Hip hinge with minimal knee bend. Lower weight to mid-shin level, drive hips through.
clinical_notes: Eccentric hamstring at long lengths — key for hamstring strain rehab.

name: Active Hamstring Stretch
description: Active hamstring stretch with neural slider.
instructions: Seated, extend leg, actively dorsiflex ankle while leaning forward at hip.
clinical_notes: Neural slider component added by ankle DF. Good for hamstring/neural sensitivity.

name: Donkey Kick
description: Isolated gluteus maximus activation in quadruped.
instructions: Quadruped, kick one leg back and up squeezing glute at top. Neutral spine.
clinical_notes: High glute max EMG. Good starting exercise for GTPS.

name: Sumo Squat
description: Wide-stance squat targeting gluteals and adductors.
instructions: Wide stance, toes out. Descend keeping knees tracking over toes.
clinical_notes: Greater glute med activation than standard squat.

name: Fire Hydrant
description: Hip abduction and ER in quadruped for glute med.
instructions: Quadruped, lift one knee out to side while keeping hip neutral.
clinical_notes: Activates posterior gluteus medius. Useful for GTPS.

name: Reverse Hyperextension (Prone)
description: Posterior chain and gluteal strengthening in prone.
instructions: Prone on elevated surface, extend both legs upward squeezing glutes. Control descent.
clinical_notes: Decompressive for lumbar while strengthening posterior chain.

name: Full Can Exercise
description: Supraspinatus strengthening in scapular plane.
instructions: Elevate arm in scapular plane (30° anterior to frontal) to 90° with thumb up. Control descent.
clinical_notes: Preferred over empty can — less subacromial compression.

name: ER with Band (Elbow at Side)
description: Infraspinatus/teres minor with resistance band.
instructions: Towel under arm, elbow 90°, pull band outward into ER. Keep elbow tucked.
clinical_notes: Towel maintains neutral shoulder position.

name: Subscapularis IR Band
description: Subscapularis strengthening with resistance band.
instructions: Elbow 90° at side, rotate forearm across body (IR) against band. Control return.
clinical_notes: Subscapularis often undertrained. Critical for balanced cuff program.

name: Rotator Cuff Progressive Overhead Loading
description: Progressive cuff loading toward overhead.
instructions: Start at 90° ABD, progress to full elevation. Use 1-2kg initially.
clinical_notes: Phase 3 rotator cuff rehab only when pain-free at lower elevations.

name: Isometric IR Doorframe
description: Subscapularis isometric against doorframe.
instructions: Elbow 90°, press hand into doorframe in IR direction. Hold.
clinical_notes: Safe early phase for post-op or acute rotator cuff.

name: PNF Diagonal D1/D2
description: Diagonal pattern for integrated rotator cuff.
instructions: D2 flexion: start low-cross-body, sweep diagonally up-out to ER. Reverse for extension.
clinical_notes: Integrates cuff in functional diagonal patterns.

name: Lateral Raise (Dumbbell)
description: Middle deltoid isolation.
instructions: Lift dumbbells out to sides to shoulder height. Lead with elbows.
clinical_notes: Avoid above 90° if impingement present.

name: Front Raise
description: Anterior deltoid strengthening.
instructions: Lift dumbbells forward to shoulder height. Control 3 sec descent.
clinical_notes: Not first choice in impingement.

name: Prone Rear Delt Fly
description: Posterior deltoid and upper back in prone.
instructions: Prone, arms hanging off bench, lift in T squeezing scapulae. Thumbs up.
clinical_notes: High posterior deltoid and periscapular activation.

name: Arnold Press
description: Full-range overhead press targeting all deltoid heads.
instructions: Start palms facing you at shoulder height, rotate to press overhead.
clinical_notes: Prerequisites: adequate shoulder mobility and rotator cuff strength.

name: Face Pull
description: Posterior deltoid and external rotator with rope.
instructions: Pull rope toward face, elbows high, finish with ER. Control return.
clinical_notes: Highly effective for posterior shoulder rehab. Low injury risk.

name: Isometric Deltoid ABD
description: Middle deltoid isometric for acute shoulder pain.
instructions: Press arm into wall at 60-90°. Hold contraction.
clinical_notes: Safe early phase. Analgesic effect established.

name: Alfredson Eccentric Heel Drop (Straight Knee)
description: Gastrocnemius eccentric — Alfredson protocol.
instructions: Rise on both feet on step, lower on affected foot below step level. 3 sec eccentric. Use other foot to return.
clinical_notes: Gold standard mid-portion Achilles tendinopathy. Continue even if painful ≤5/10.

name: Alfredson Eccentric Heel Drop (Bent Knee)
description: Soleus eccentric — Alfredson bent-knee.
instructions: Same as straight-knee but with 20° knee bend throughout. Isolates soleus.
clinical_notes: Combined with straight-knee for complete Alfredson protocol.

name: Seated Calf Raise (Soleus)
description: Isolated soleus strengthening.
instructions: Seated knees 90°, rise onto toes under load (plate on thigh). Control descent.
clinical_notes: For insertional Achilles (soleus dominant). Seated = low Achilles compression.

name: Calf Isometric Hold (Achilles)
description: Isometric calf for Achilles pain management.
instructions: Single leg on step, hold isometric mid-range plantarflexion for 45 sec.
clinical_notes: Immediate analgesic effect for Achilles pain.

name: Plantarflexion Resisted Band
description: Full plantarflexion strengthening with band.
instructions: Seated, band around forefoot. Plantarflex through full range. Control return.
clinical_notes: Early phase ankle rehab and post-immobilisation.

name: Proprioceptive Single-Leg Calf Raise
description: Balance + calf strength on single leg.
instructions: Single leg calf raise on balance disc. Slow controlled movement.
clinical_notes: Combines calf strength with ankle proprioception.

name: Supine Iliopsoas Stretch
description: Modified Thomas stretch — safe for LBP.
instructions: Supine, hug one knee, let other leg extend flat. Feel anterior hip stretch.
clinical_notes: Maintains lumbar spine stability during stretch.

name: Resisted Hip Flexion (Seated)
description: Isolated iliopsoas strengthening.
instructions: Seated, band around thigh. Lift knee against resistance to 90°. Control descent.
clinical_notes: For iliopsoas tendinopathy and post-op hip rehab.

name: TFL Stretch (Side-lying)
description: Side-lying stretch for TFL.
instructions: Side-lying, bottom leg forward. Drop top leg behind and sink toward floor. Feel lateral thigh stretch.
clinical_notes: Targets TFL belly more effectively than standing Ober stretch.

name: Dead Bug with Resisted Hip Flexion
description: Core stability with hip flexor activation.
instructions: Dead bug position, push knee into band at 90° while arm extends overhead.
clinical_notes: Integrates hip flexor with core stability.

name: Mountain Climber
description: Dynamic hip flexor and core activation.
instructions: Push-up position, drive alternate knees toward chest in running motion.
clinical_notes: Dynamic hip flexor activation with anterior core loading.

name: Serratus Anterior Wall Slide
description: Serratus strengthening in wall contact.
instructions: Forearms on wall, protract scapulae, slide arms up maintaining protraction.
clinical_notes: High serratus activation. Key for scapular winging.

name: Seated Band Row
description: Mid trap and rhomboid strengthening.
instructions: Pull band toward lower abdomen, squeeze scapulae at end range. Elbows near body.
clinical_notes: Horizontal pull essential in shoulder rehab.

name: Lower Trap Cable Pull
description: Targeted lower trapezius on cable.
instructions: Reach overhead to cable, pull diagonally down and backward. Arm at 120°.
clinical_notes: Isolated lower trap in functional direction.

name: Rhomboid Pinch
description: Isolated rhomboid and mid-trap activation.
instructions: Squeeze scapulae together as if holding pencil between them. Hold 5 sec.
clinical_notes: Simple postural correction. Pairs with pec minor stretching.

name: Superman Hold
description: Bilateral erector spinae and glute strengthening.
instructions: Prone, lift both arms and legs simultaneously. Hold 2-3 sec at top.
clinical_notes: Lumbar extensor endurance. Progression from bird dog.

name: Back Extension (Roman Chair)
description: Loaded erector spinae and posterior chain.
instructions: On hyperextension bench, hinge at hip. Extend to neutral (not hyperextension).
clinical_notes: Strong evidence for chronic LBP. Add load as strength improves.

name: Lumbar Rotation Stretch
description: Lumbar rotation mobility stretch.
instructions: Supine, knees bent, rotate both knees to one side. Look opposite direction.
clinical_notes: Gentle lumbar rotation. Appropriate all phases of LBP.

name: Prone Press-Up (Cobra)
description: Lumbar extension to mobilise and stretch.
instructions: Prone, hands under shoulders, push chest up leaving pelvis on floor.
clinical_notes: McKenzie extension when extension centralises LBP.

name: Adductor Isometric Squeeze
description: Isometric adductor activation.
instructions: Supine, knees bent, squeeze pillow between knees at 45°. Hold 5 sec.
clinical_notes: Early pain relief in groin/osteitis pubis. Safe acute phase.

name: Side-lying Hip Adduction
description: Isolated adductor strengthening.
instructions: Bottom leg straight, top leg on pillow/chair. Lift bottom leg up. Control descent.
clinical_notes: Isolated adductor strengthening. Progress to weighted.

name: Butterfly Adductor Stretch
description: Hip adductor flexibility.
instructions: Seated, soles together, press knees toward floor gently.
clinical_notes: For groin strain and hip mobility.

name: Sumo Deadlift
description: Wide-stance deadlift with adductor engagement.
instructions: Wide stance, toes out. Hinge and squat, drive through heels. Chest up.
clinical_notes: High adductor magnus activation for groin rehab return to loading.

name: Lateral Lunge (Eccentric Adductor)
description: Eccentric adductor loading in lateral lunge.
instructions: Step wide to side, lower into lateral lunge feeling adductor stretch on straight leg. Control return.
clinical_notes: Eccentric adductor at long lengths. Key for groin strain rehab.

name: Cable Hip Adduction
description: Standing cable adduction for functional strengthening.
instructions: Stand beside low cable, cross working leg in front of supporting leg. Control return.
clinical_notes: Mimics kicking motion for athletes.

name: Cervical Extension Strengthening (Prone)
description: Neck extensor strengthening against gravity.
instructions: Prone on table, head off edge. Slowly extend head to neutral. Control descent.
clinical_notes: For WAD and cervical rehab. Start gravity-only.

name: SCM Stretch
description: Sternocleidomastoid stretching.
instructions: Lateral flex head away, rotate toward same side, extend slightly. Press down on clavicle.
clinical_notes: For cervicogenic headache and TOS.

name: Scalene Stretch
description: Anterior and middle scalene stretch.
instructions: Lateral flex head away from tight side, gentle overpressure. Depress shoulder on same side.
clinical_notes: Critical for TOS and cervicogenic headache.

name: Upper Trap Stretch
description: Upper trapezius and levator scapulae stretch.
instructions: Lateral flex toward shoulder, hand over head applying gentle overpressure. Keep opposite shoulder depressed.
clinical_notes: For cervicogenic headache and neck pain.

name: Cervical Flexion Strengthening
description: Neck flexor strength training against gravity.
instructions: Supine, chin tuck first then lift head 2-3cm. Maintain tuck throughout.
clinical_notes: For WAD and chronic neck pain.

name: 4-Way Cervical Isometric
description: Isometric cervical strengthening in 4 planes.
instructions: Apply gentle hand resistance to forehead, occiput, and each side. Resist without movement. Hold.
clinical_notes: Safe cervical strengthening. No shear or compressive stress.

name: Splenius Capitis Stretch
description: Neck rotation stretch targeting splenius capitis.
instructions: Flex head forward, rotate 30° away from tight side, gentle overpressure at occiput.
clinical_notes: For posterior neck pain and cervicogenic headache.

name: Biceps Slow Eccentric Curl
description: Eccentric biceps for tendinopathy.
instructions: Curl up with both arms, lower on one arm 4 seconds. Supinated grip.
clinical_notes: For distal biceps tendinopathy.

name: Dumbbell Pronation/Supination
description: Forearm rotator strengthening.
instructions: Elbow 90°, hold dumbbell at end, rotate forearm pronation to supination. Control.
clinical_notes: For lateral/medial epicondylitis and forearm rehab.

name: Wrist Extension HSR
description: Heavy slow resistance wrist extension.
instructions: Forearm supported, wrist extension with dumbbell 3 sec up, 3 sec down. Full ROM.
clinical_notes: HSR comparable to eccentric alone for lateral epicondylitis.

name: Median Nerve Tendon Glides
description: Median nerve mobilisation for carpal tunnel.
instructions: 6-position tendon glide: straight, hook, fist, tabletop, straight knuckle, OK sign. Gentle.
clinical_notes: Reduces neural tension and synovial adhesion in CTS.

name: Ulnar Nerve Gliding
description: Ulnar nerve mobilisation for cubital tunnel.
instructions: Extend wrist and abduct little finger, extend elbow, then flex elbow maintaining positions.
clinical_notes: For cubital tunnel. Slider technique first.

name: Wrist Flexion Eccentric
description: Eccentric wrist flexion for medial epicondylitis.
instructions: Use other hand to flex wrist, lower eccentrically with affected side. 4 sec descent.
clinical_notes: For medial epicondylitis progressive loading.

name: Rubber Band Finger Extension
description: Extensor digitorum strengthening.
instructions: Loop rubber band around fingers, spread fingers against resistance. Control return.
clinical_notes: Balances flexor dominance. For CTS, trigger finger, hand rehab.

name: Grip Strengthening (Putty/Ball)
description: Grip force and forearm strengthening.
instructions: Squeeze putty or grip device with full force. Hold 5 sec, release.
clinical_notes: For CTS, trigger finger, and general hand rehabilitation.

name: Chin Tuck
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Deep Cervical Flexors, Longus Colli in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Retract chin straight back creating a double chin. Hold 5 seconds.
clinical_notes: Gold standard for cervical stabilisation. Jull et al. 2008.

name: Chin Tuck with Resistance
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Deep Cervical Flexors, Longus Colli in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Perform chin tuck against resistance band placed behind head.
clinical_notes: Progression from basic chin tucks. Increases deep flexor endurance.

name: Deep Neck Flexor Endurance Hold
description: Isometric exercise targeting Deep Cervical Flexors in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Supine chin tuck, lift head 1 inch off table. Hold as long as possible.
clinical_notes: Test and train. Normal >30s. Reduced in neck pain (Jull 2008).

name: Cervical Rotation AROM
description: Mobility exercise targeting SCM, Splenius Capitis in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Slowly turn head to look over each shoulder. Return to centre.
clinical_notes: Maintain full cervical ROM. Avoid end-range forcing in acute pain.

name: Upper Trapezius Stretch
description: Stretching exercise targeting Upper Trapezius in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Sit tall, tilt head away, gently apply overpressure. Depress opposite shoulder.
clinical_notes: Essential for tension-type headache and forward head posture.

name: Levator Scapulae Stretch
description: Stretching exercise targeting Levator Scapulae in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Look into armpit, apply gentle overpressure. Sit on hand to depress shoulder.
clinical_notes: Stretch levator by rotating and flexing cervical spine.

name: Cervical Isometric Flexion
description: Isometric exercise targeting Deep Cervical Flexors, SCM in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Place hand on forehead, push head into hand without movement.
clinical_notes: Safe for acute neck pain. Activates stabilisers without movement.

name: Cervical Isometric Extension
description: Isometric exercise targeting Semispinalis, Splenius Capitis in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Place hand on back of head, push head backward without movement.
clinical_notes: Pair with isometric flexion for balanced cervical strengthening.

name: Cervical Isometric Lateral Flexion
description: Isometric exercise targeting Scalenes, Upper Trapezius in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Place hand on side of head, push laterally without movement.
clinical_notes: Isometric lateral flexion strengthening. Safe in acute phase.

name: Cervical Isometric Rotation
description: Isometric exercise targeting SCM, Splenius Capitis in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Place hand on temple, attempt to rotate without movement.
clinical_notes: Completes multi-directional isometric cervical programme.

name: Suboccipital Release Self-Massage
description: Mobility exercise targeting Suboccipitals in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Lie supine on two tennis balls at base of skull. Allow weight to release tension.
clinical_notes: Effective for cervicogenic headache. Avoid excessive pressure.

name: Cervical Retraction with Extension
description: Mobility exercise targeting Deep Cervical Flexors, Suboccipitals in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Perform chin tuck then gently extend neck while maintaining retraction.
clinical_notes: McKenzie-based. Centralisation principle for disc pathology.

name: Scapular Setting with Cervical Retraction
description: Functional exercise targeting Deep Cervical Flexors, Lower Trapezius in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Retract chin and simultaneously set scapulae down and back.
clinical_notes: Integrates cervico-scapular stabilisation. Key for postural retraining.

name: Cervical Flexion-Rotation Test Exercise
description: Mobility exercise targeting Suboccipitals, Deep Cervical Flexors in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: In full cervical flexion, rotate head left and right to end range.
clinical_notes: Based on FRT test. Targets C1-2 rotation. Key for cervicogenic headache.

name: Thoracic Extension with Foam Roller
description: Mobility exercise targeting Thoracic Extensors in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Lie over foam roller at mid-thoracic level. Extend over roller.
clinical_notes: Improves thoracic mobility, reduces cervical compensatory stress.

name: Neck Stabilisation Ball Exercise
description: Functional exercise targeting Deep Cervical Flexors, SCM in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Press forehead into small ball against wall while performing chin tuck. Move in patterns.
clinical_notes: Advanced neuromuscular control exercise for cervical spine.

name: SNAG C1-C2 Self-Mobilisation
description: Mobility exercise targeting Suboccipitals in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Use towel around C1-C2, apply sustained glide while rotating.
clinical_notes: Based on Mulligan concept. Strong evidence for cervicogenic headache.

name: Prone Cervical Retraction
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Deep Cervical Flexors, Cervical Extensors in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Lie prone with forehead on towel. Perform chin tuck lifting head slightly.
clinical_notes: Gravity-assisted deep flexor strengthening in prone.

name: Cervical Proprioception Laser Exercise
description: Balance exercise targeting Deep Cervical Flexors, Cervical Muscles in the Cervical Spine region.
instructions: Attach laser to headband, trace patterns on wall with head movements.
clinical_notes: Cervicocephalic kinesthetic sense training. Key for WAD and dizziness.

name: Prone Y Raise
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Lower Trapezius in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Lie prone, raise arms in Y position with thumbs up.
clinical_notes: Excellent lower trap activation. Essential for scapular dyskinesis.

name: Prone T Raise
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Middle Trapezius, Rhomboids in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Lie prone, raise arms in T position with thumbs up.
clinical_notes: Targets middle trapezius and rhomboids. Pair with Y and W raises.

name: Prone W Raise
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Lower Trapezius, Infraspinatus in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Lie prone, arms in W position, squeeze shoulder blades and lift.
clinical_notes: Combined external rotation and scapular retraction.

name: Serratus Anterior Push-Up Plus
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Serratus Anterior in the Shoulder region.
instructions: At top of push-up, protract scapulae further (plus phase).
clinical_notes: Highest serratus anterior EMG activity (Decker 1999).

name: Shoulder Flexion with Theraband
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Anterior Deltoid, Supraspinatus in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Stand on band, raise arm forward to shoulder height.
clinical_notes: Early-stage shoulder strengthening. Control eccentric phase.

name: Internal Rotation with Band
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Subscapularis in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Elbow at side at 90°, rotate forearm inward against band.
clinical_notes: Subscapularis strengthening. Maintain towel roll under elbow.

name: External Rotation at 90° Abduction
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Infraspinatus, Teres Minor in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Arm at 90° abduction, elbow at 90°, rotate forearm upward.
clinical_notes: Functional position for overhead athletes. Progress from side-lying ER.

name: Sleeper Stretch
description: Stretching exercise targeting Posterior Capsule, Infraspinatus in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Side-lie on involved side, arm at 90°, internally rotate with other hand.
clinical_notes: Addresses posterior shoulder tightness/GIRD. Gentle pressure only.

name: Cross-Body Adduction Stretch
description: Stretching exercise targeting Posterior Deltoid, Infraspinatus in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Pull arm across body at shoulder height with opposite hand.
clinical_notes: Alternative to sleeper stretch for posterior tightness.

name: Pec Minor Stretch (Doorway)
description: Stretching exercise targeting Pectoralis Minor in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Place forearm on doorframe at 90°, step through. Avoid anterior shoulder pain.
clinical_notes: Essential for rounded shoulder posture correction.

name: Pendulum Exercise (Codman)
description: Mobility exercise targeting Shoulder Complex in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Lean forward, let arm hang. Create small circles using body momentum.
clinical_notes: Grade I-II mobilisation effect. First exercise post-surgery/acute injury.

name: Wall Walk (Finger Ladder)
description: Mobility exercise targeting Shoulder Flexors in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Face wall, walk fingers up as high as tolerable.
clinical_notes: Progressive shoulder flexion ROM. Monitor for compensatory hiking.

name: Shoulder PNF D1 Flexion
description: Functional exercise targeting Shoulder Complex in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Diagonal pattern: opposite hip to overhead, combining flexion/adduction/ER.
clinical_notes: PNF diagonal for functional shoulder strengthening.

name: Shoulder PNF D2 Flexion
description: Functional exercise targeting Shoulder Complex in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Diagonal pattern: opposite hip to overhead, combining flexion/abduction/ER.
clinical_notes: Sport-specific pattern. Mimics overhead throwing pattern.

name: Rhythmic Stabilisation Shoulder
description: Isometric exercise targeting Rotator Cuff in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Partner applies alternating perturbations while patient holds arm position.
clinical_notes: Neuromuscular control training. Key for instability rehab.

name: Shoulder CKC Weight Bearing
description: Functional exercise targeting Rotator Cuff, Serratus Anterior in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Weight bear through hands on table. Shift weight side to side.
clinical_notes: Closed kinetic chain stabilisation. Joint compression effect.

name: Turkish Get-Up (Modified)
description: Functional exercise targeting Shoulder Complex, Core in the Shoulder region.
instructions: From supine, rise to standing while keeping weight overhead. Modify as needed.
clinical_notes: Advanced total-body shoulder stabilisation exercise.

name: Scapular Clock Exercise
description: Functional exercise targeting Serratus Anterior, Trapezius in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Hand on wall, move body to create scapular protraction/retraction/elevation/depression.
clinical_notes: Dynamic scapular control in weight-bearing position.

name: Lat Pulldown (Scapular Focus)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Latissimus Dorsi, Lower Trapezius in the Shoulder region.
instructions: Focus on scapular depression and retraction during lat pulldown.
clinical_notes: Functional pulling pattern with scapular emphasis.

name: Eccentric Wrist Extension
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Wrist Extensors in the Elbow/Wrist/Hand region.
instructions: Support forearm, slowly lower weight from wrist extension to flexion.
clinical_notes: Gold standard for lateral epicondylalgia. Stasinopoulos 2005.

name: Eccentric Wrist Flexion
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Wrist Flexors in the Elbow/Wrist/Hand region.
instructions: Support forearm palm up, slowly lower weight from flexion to extension.
clinical_notes: Evidence-based treatment for medial epicondylalgia.

name: Tyler Twist (FlexBar)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Wrist Extensors in the Elbow/Wrist/Hand region.
instructions: Twist FlexBar using eccentric wrist extension. Controlled release.
clinical_notes: 78% improvement vs 72% with eccentric alone (Tyler 2010).

name: Reverse Tyler Twist
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Wrist Flexors in the Elbow/Wrist/Hand region.
instructions: Twist FlexBar using eccentric wrist flexion (reverse direction).
clinical_notes: Adapted for golfer's elbow. Eccentric flexor loading.

name: Grip Strengthening
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Hand Intrinsics, Forearm Flexors in the Elbow/Wrist/Hand region.
instructions: Squeeze tennis ball or hand gripper. Hold 5 seconds.
clinical_notes: Progressive grip strengthening. Monitor for pain.

name: Finger Extension with Band
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Finger Extensors in the Elbow/Wrist/Hand region.
instructions: Place rubber band around fingers, spread fingers apart against resistance.
clinical_notes: Balances flexor/extensor strength ratio. Important for grip rehab.

name: Wrist Pronation/Supination
description: Mobility exercise targeting Forearm Pronators, Forearm Supinators in the Elbow/Wrist/Hand region.
instructions: Hold hammer or weight, rotate forearm fully pronation to supination.
clinical_notes: Restores forearm rotation. Progress with increasing lever arm.

name: Tendon Gliding Exercises
description: Mobility exercise targeting Hand Intrinsics in the Elbow/Wrist/Hand region.
instructions: Sequential positions: straight, hook, fist, tabletop, straight fist.
clinical_notes: Essential for carpal tunnel syndrome and post-surgery tendon rehab.

name: Nerve Gliding - Median Nerve
description: Mobility exercise targeting Median Nerve Path in the Elbow/Wrist/Hand region.
instructions: Sequential positions progressing from wrist neutral to full extension with finger extension.
clinical_notes: Totten & Hunter technique. Evidence for CTS management.

name: Nerve Gliding - Ulnar Nerve
description: Mobility exercise targeting Ulnar Nerve Path in the Elbow/Wrist/Hand region.
instructions: Elbow flexion/extension with wrist in neutral, progress to wrist extension.
clinical_notes: For cubital tunnel syndrome. Avoid end-range stretching if irritable.

name: Nerve Gliding - Radial Nerve
description: Mobility exercise targeting Radial Nerve Path in the Elbow/Wrist/Hand region.
instructions: Shoulder depression, IR, wrist flexion, forearm pronation sequence.
clinical_notes: For radial tunnel syndrome. Gentle tensioning only.

name: Thumb Opposition Exercise
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Thenar Group in the Elbow/Wrist/Hand region.
instructions: Touch thumb tip to each fingertip, making an O shape. Add resistance.
clinical_notes: Thenar strengthening for CMC/1st MCP conditions.

name: Putty Exercises - Pinch Patterns
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Hand Intrinsics in the Elbow/Wrist/Hand region.
instructions: Use therapy putty: lateral pinch, tip pinch, palmar pinch, full grip.
clinical_notes: Graduated hand strengthening. Match putty resistance to tolerance.

name: Wrist Dart Thrower's Motion
description: Functional exercise targeting Wrist Extensors, Wrist Flexors in the Elbow/Wrist/Hand region.
instructions: Move wrist from radial extension to ulnar flexion in dart-throwing arc.
clinical_notes: Functional wrist pattern. Most stable column of carpus.

name: Cat-Cow (Thoracic Focus)
description: Mobility exercise targeting Thoracic Extensors, Thoracic Flexors in the Thoracic Spine region.
instructions: On all fours, arch and round thoracic spine specifically. Minimise lumbar movement.
clinical_notes: Segmental thoracic mobility. Cue patient to focus motion at mid-back.

name: Thread the Needle
description: Mobility exercise targeting Thoracic Rotators in the Thoracic Spine region.
instructions: On all fours, reach one arm under body rotating thorax. Then reach to ceiling.
clinical_notes: Thoracic rotation mobility. Maintain neutral lumbar spine.

name: Open Book (Side-Lying Rotation)
description: Mobility exercise targeting Thoracic Rotators, Pectorals in the Thoracic Spine region.
instructions: Side-lying, knees bent, rotate upper body opening chest to ceiling.
clinical_notes: Combined thoracic rotation and pectoral stretch.

name: Prone Thoracic Extension (Superman)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Thoracic Extensors, Multifidus in the Thoracic Spine region.
instructions: Lie prone, hands at temples, lift chest off surface using thoracic extensors.
clinical_notes: Strengthens thoracic extensors against gravity. Avoid lumbar hyperextension.

name: Seated Thoracic Rotation
description: Mobility exercise targeting Thoracic Rotators in the Thoracic Spine region.
instructions: Sit straddling bench, arms crossed, rotate thorax left and right.
clinical_notes: Isolates thoracic rotation by fixing pelvis.

name: Thoracic Self-Mobilisation (TPAM)
description: Mobility exercise targeting Thoracic Spine in the Thoracic Spine region.
instructions: Use double tennis ball or peanut tool along paravertebral muscles. Apply PA pressure.
clinical_notes: Emulates PA mobilisation. Grade III-IV effects.

name: Wall Angel
description: Functional exercise targeting Lower Trapezius, Serratus Anterior in the Thoracic Spine region.
instructions: Stand with back against wall. Slide arms up and down maintaining contact.
clinical_notes: Combines thoracic extension with scapular control. Great for posture.

name: Prone I-Y-T-W Series
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Lower Trapezius, Middle Trapezius in the Thoracic Spine region.
instructions: Lie prone. Perform raises in I, Y, T, and W positions sequentially.
clinical_notes: Comprehensive scapulothoracic strengthening protocol.

name: Rib Expansion Breathing
description: Mobility exercise targeting Intercostals, Diaphragm in the Thoracic Spine region.
instructions: Place hands on ribs. Breathe into hands expanding ribcage laterally.
clinical_notes: Thoracic cage mobility via breath work. Useful for costal restrictions.

name: Transversus Abdominis Activation
description: Isometric exercise targeting Transversus Abdominis in the Lumbar Spine region.
instructions: Supine, draw lower abdomen inward gently. Maintain breathing. Hold 10 seconds.
clinical_notes: Foundation for lumbar stabilisation. Richardson et al. Motor control approach.

name: Multifidus Activation (Prone)
description: Isometric exercise targeting Multifidus in the Lumbar Spine region.
instructions: Lie prone, palpate multifidus adjacent to spinous process. Gently swell muscle.
clinical_notes: Isolated multifidus retraining. Evidence for recurrence prevention.

name: Modified Curl-Up (McGill)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Rectus Abdominis, External Obliques in the Lumbar Spine region.
instructions: Supine, one knee bent, hands under lumbar spine. Lift head/shoulders only.
clinical_notes: McGill Big 3. Spares the spine while training abdominals.

name: Side Plank (McGill)
description: Isometric exercise targeting Obliques, Quadratus Lumborum in the Lumbar Spine region.
instructions: Side-lying, prop on elbow. Lift hips creating straight line. Hold.
clinical_notes: McGill Big 3. Excellent lateral stabiliser with minimal spinal load.

name: Prone Press-Up (McKenzie Extension)
description: Mobility exercise targeting Lumbar Extensors in the Lumbar Spine region.
instructions: Lie prone, press up on hands extending lumbar spine. Hips stay on surface.
clinical_notes: McKenzie extension principle. First-line for disc-related LBP.

name: Lumbar Flexion in Lying
description: Mobility exercise targeting Lumbar Flexors in the Lumbar Spine region.
instructions: Supine, pull both knees to chest. Hold gently.
clinical_notes: Flexion-based approach. Useful for spinal stenosis and facet irritation.

name: Pelvic Tilt
description: Mobility exercise targeting Transversus Abdominis, Rectus Abdominis in the Lumbar Spine region.
instructions: Supine, flatten low back against surface by tilting pelvis posteriorly.
clinical_notes: Foundation exercise for lumbar motor control. Teach before progression.

name: Supine Bridge
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gluteus Maximus, Multifidus in the Lumbar Spine region.
instructions: Supine with knees bent. Lift hips off surface creating straight line from shoulders to knees.
clinical_notes: Bridges gluteal and lumbar stabiliser activation.

name: Single Leg Bridge
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gluteus Maximus, Multifidus in the Lumbar Spine region.
instructions: Bridge with one leg extended. Maintain level pelvis.
clinical_notes: Progression from bilateral bridge. Challenges pelvic stability.

name: QL Stretch (Side-Lying)
description: Stretching exercise targeting Quadratus Lumborum in the Lumbar Spine region.
instructions: Side-lying over foam roller at waist level. Reach overhead.
clinical_notes: Quadratus lumborum stretch. Useful for lateral shift or lateral LBP.

name: Lumbar Segmental Rotation
description: Mobility exercise targeting Lumbar Rotators in the Lumbar Spine region.
instructions: Supine, knees bent. Rotate knees side to side within comfortable range.
clinical_notes: Gentle lumbar rotation. Useful for general stiffness.

name: Clamshell
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gluteus Medius in the Hip region.
instructions: Side-lying, knees bent, feet together. Open top knee like a clamshell.
clinical_notes: Low-load glute med activation. Place band above knees for progression.

name: Side-Lying Hip Abduction
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gluteus Medius, Gluteus Minimus in the Hip region.
instructions: Side-lying, lift top leg to 30° keeping knee straight. Control lowering.
clinical_notes: Higher glute med EMG than clamshell (Distefano 2009).

name: Single Leg Squat
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gluteus Medius, Quadriceps in the Hip region.
instructions: Stand on one leg, squat to 60° knee flexion maintaining knee over toes.
clinical_notes: Functional test and exercise. Monitors dynamic knee valgus.

name: Monster Walk
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gluteus Medius, TFL in the Hip region.
instructions: Band around ankles, quarter squat, walk sideways maintaining squat depth.
clinical_notes: Dynamic glute med strengthening. Essential for hip stabilisation.

name: Hip Flexor Stretch (Thomas Position)
description: Stretching exercise targeting Iliopsoas, Rectus Femoris in the Hip region.
instructions: Lie at edge of table, pull one knee to chest, let other leg hang.
clinical_notes: Gold standard hip flexor flexibility assessment and stretch.

name: Piriformis Stretch (Figure 4)
description: Stretching exercise targeting Piriformis in the Hip region.
instructions: Supine, cross ankle over opposite knee. Pull bottom knee toward chest.
clinical_notes: Address piriformis tightness. Differentiate from sciatic nerve tension.

name: Standing Hip Abduction with Band
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gluteus Medius in the Hip region.
instructions: Stand on one leg, move other leg outward against band resistance.
clinical_notes: Standing functional glute med strengthening.

name: Hip Hike Exercise
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gluteus Medius, Quadratus Lumborum in the Hip region.
instructions: Stand on step, drop uninvolved pelvis, then hike up using stance-side glute med.
clinical_notes: Targets pelvic control. Key for Trendelenburg correction.

name: Prone Hip Extension
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gluteus Maximus in the Hip region.
instructions: Lie prone, bend knee to 90°, lift thigh off surface. Squeeze glute.
clinical_notes: Isolates gluteus maximus. Monitor for hamstring substitution.

name: Adductor Squeeze
description: Isometric exercise targeting Adductors in the Hip region.
instructions: Supine with ball between knees. Squeeze ball and hold.
clinical_notes: Adductor strengthening. Key for groin pain and pelvic stability.

name: Banded Lateral Walk
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gluteus Medius, Gluteus Minimus in the Hip region.
instructions: Band around ankles, quarter squat position, walk laterally.
clinical_notes: Dynamic hip abductor strengthening with functional carry-over.

name: Straight Leg Raise
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Quadriceps, Hip Flexors in the Knee region.
instructions: Supine, tighten quads, lift straight leg to 30-45°.
clinical_notes: Early post-operative exercise. Maintain quad activation throughout.

name: Terminal Knee Extension
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Quadriceps (VMO) in the Knee region.
instructions: Band behind knee, from 30° flexion extend to full. Focus on VMO.
clinical_notes: Targets last 30° of extension. Key for patellar tracking.

name: Step-Down Exercise
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Quadriceps, Gluteus Medius in the Knee region.
instructions: Stand on step, slowly lower opposite heel to floor. Control descent.
clinical_notes: Eccentric quad strengthening. Monitor frontal plane knee control.

name: Hamstring Curl (Prone)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Hamstrings in the Knee region.
instructions: Prone, curl heel toward buttock against resistance.
clinical_notes: Basic hamstring strengthening. Control eccentric phase.

name: Wall Sit
description: Isometric exercise targeting Quadriceps in the Knee region.
instructions: Back against wall, slide to 60° knee flexion. Hold position.
clinical_notes: Isometric quad strengthening. Adjust angle to avoid painful arc.

name: Spanish Squat
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Quadriceps, Patellar Tendon in the Knee region.
instructions: Band behind knees attached to pole. Lean back and squat.
clinical_notes: Excellent for patellar tendinopathy. Reduces PFJ compression.

name: VMO Dip
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Quadriceps (VMO) in the Knee region.
instructions: Stand on step on affected leg. Bend to 30-45° and return.
clinical_notes: Functional VMO strengthening. Eccentric emphasis on descent.

name: Heel Slide
description: Mobility exercise targeting Knee Flexors/Extensors in the Knee region.
instructions: Supine, slide heel toward buttock bending knee. Assist with towel if needed.
clinical_notes: Early post-operative ROM exercise. Progress range gradually.

name: Patellar Mobilisation
description: Mobility exercise targeting Patellar Region in the Knee region.
instructions: Gently glide patella medially, laterally, superiorly, and inferiorly.
clinical_notes: Essential for patellar mobility. Focus on restricted direction.

name: Single-Leg Press
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Quadriceps, Gluteus Maximus in the Knee region.
instructions: Single leg press at 60-90° ROM. Control eccentric phase.
clinical_notes: Unilateral strengthening. Address bilateral deficit.

name: Heel Raise (Bilateral)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gastrocnemius, Soleus in the Ankle/Foot region.
instructions: Stand on edge of step, raise up on toes. Slowly lower below step level.
clinical_notes: Basic calf strengthening. Eccentric emphasis for tendinopathy.

name: Single-Leg Heel Raise
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gastrocnemius, Soleus in the Ankle/Foot region.
instructions: Single leg on step edge. Rise up and lower slowly.
clinical_notes: Alfredson protocol adaptation. Must do eccentric for Achilles tendinopathy.

name: Soleus Heel Raise (Bent Knee)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Soleus in the Ankle/Foot region.
instructions: Seated or standing with knee bent 20-30°, perform heel raise.
clinical_notes: Isolates soleus (gastrocnemius shortened). Key for soleus-dominant pathology.

name: Ankle Alphabet
description: Mobility exercise targeting Ankle Muscles in the Ankle/Foot region.
instructions: Trace alphabet with foot using ankle movements.
clinical_notes: Multi-directional ankle mobility. Good for early rehab.

name: Ankle Dorsiflexion Wall Stretch
description: Stretching exercise targeting Gastrocnemius, Soleus in the Ankle/Foot region.
instructions: Face wall, foot flat, lean forward driving knee over toes.
clinical_notes: Address ankle dorsiflexion deficit. <10cm on WBLT is restricted.

name: Single-Leg Balance
description: Balance exercise targeting Ankle Stabilisers, Peroneals in the Ankle/Foot region.
instructions: Stand on one leg, maintain balance. Progress: eyes closed, unstable surface.
clinical_notes: Foundation of ankle proprioception training. Start on firm surface.

name: BOSU Ball Balance
description: Balance exercise targeting Ankle Stabilisers in the Ankle/Foot region.
instructions: Stand on BOSU ball (flat side up or dome side up). Maintain balance.
clinical_notes: Advanced proprioception. Progress to eyes closed and perturbations.

name: Tibialis Posterior Strengthening
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Tibialis Posterior in the Ankle/Foot region.
instructions: Sit with band around forefoot. Invert and plantarflex against resistance.
clinical_notes: Key for posterior tibial tendon dysfunction and flat foot correction.

name: Peroneal Strengthening (Eversion)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Peroneals in the Ankle/Foot region.
instructions: Sit with band around forefoot. Evert foot against resistance.
clinical_notes: Essential after ankle sprain. Dynamic lateral ankle stability.

name: Short Foot Exercise
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Intrinsic Foot Muscles in the Ankle/Foot region.
instructions: While seated/standing, shorten foot by raising arch without curling toes.
clinical_notes: Intrinsic foot muscle activation. Key for foot core training (McKeon 2015).

name: Toe Yoga
description: Mobility exercise targeting Intrinsic Foot Muscles in the Ankle/Foot region.
instructions: Lift big toe while pressing others down, then reverse. Alternate.
clinical_notes: Intrinsic foot muscle isolation and neural control.

name: Ankle Inversion/Eversion Proprioception
description: Balance exercise targeting Peroneals, Tibialis Posterior in the Ankle/Foot region.
instructions: Stand on tilt board or wobble board. Control ankle position.
clinical_notes: Dynamic ankle stability training. Strong evidence for sprain prevention.

name: Achilles Eccentric Decline Board
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gastrocnemius, Soleus in the Ankle/Foot region.
instructions: Stand on decline board (25°), perform eccentric heel lowering on single leg.
clinical_notes: Alfredson protocol. Strong evidence for mid-portion Achilles tendinopathy.

name: Diaphragmatic Breathing
description: Functional exercise targeting Diaphragm in the Core region.
instructions: Supine, hands on chest and abdomen. Breathe into abdomen without chest rise.
clinical_notes: Foundation of core control. Establishes IAP regulation.

name: Kegel Exercise
description: Isometric exercise targeting Pelvic Floor in the Core region.
instructions: Contract pelvic floor as if stopping urine flow. Hold 5-10 seconds.
clinical_notes: Level 1 evidence for stress urinary incontinence. Bo et al. 2017.

name: Anti-Extension Hold (Body Saw)
description: Isometric exercise targeting Core Muscles in the Core region.
instructions: Plank position on sliders. Rock forward and backward maintaining neutral spine.
clinical_notes: Advanced anti-extension challenge. Significant core demand.

name: Half Kneeling Chop
description: Functional exercise targeting Obliques, Core in the Core region.
instructions: Half kneeling, pull cable/band diagonally across body. Control rotation.
clinical_notes: PNF-based functional core pattern. Anti-rotation and rotation control.

name: Half Kneeling Lift
description: Functional exercise targeting Obliques, Core in the Core region.
instructions: Half kneeling, push cable/band diagonally upward across body.
clinical_notes: Complementary pattern to chop. Integrates hip and core stability.

name: Stir the Pot
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Core Muscles in the Core region.
instructions: Forearms on stability ball in plank. Make circular motions with forearms.
clinical_notes: Stuart McGill recommended. High core activation with minimal spine load.

name: Farmers Carry
description: Functional exercise targeting Core, Grip in the Core region.
instructions: Walk with heavy weights in each hand maintaining upright posture.
clinical_notes: Functional total-body stabilisation. Anti-lateral flexion demand.

name: Squat
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Quadriceps, Gluteus Maximus in the Full Body region.
instructions: Feet shoulder width. Sit back and down to 90° knee flexion. Drive through heels.
clinical_notes: Fundamental movement pattern. Assess for compensation patterns.

name: Deadlift (Hip Hinge)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Posterior Chain, Core in the Full Body region.
instructions: Hinge at hips with neutral spine. Drive hips forward to stand.
clinical_notes: Fundamental posterior chain pattern. Teach neutral spine first.

name: Lunge (Forward)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Quadriceps, Gluteus Maximus in the Full Body region.
instructions: Step forward into lunge, lower until back knee nearly touches floor.
clinical_notes: Unilateral lower extremity strengthening. Monitor knee valgus.

name: Step-Up
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Quadriceps, Gluteus Maximus in the Full Body region.
instructions: Step up onto box/step, fully extend stance leg at top.
clinical_notes: Functional unilateral exercise. Adjust height to challenge level.

name: Push-Up
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Pectorals, Triceps in the Full Body region.
instructions: Standard push-up with neutral spine. Modify on knees or wall if needed.
clinical_notes: Upper body pushing pattern. CKC shoulder stabilisation.

name: Row (Band/Dumbbell)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Rhomboids, Middle Trapezius in the Full Body region.
instructions: Pull band or dumbbell toward hip, squeezing shoulder blades.
clinical_notes: Upper body pulling pattern. Essential for postural correction.

name: Bear Crawl
description: Functional exercise targeting Core, Shoulders in the Full Body region.
instructions: Crawl forward on hands and feet, knees hovering 1 inch off ground.
clinical_notes: Integrated locomotion pattern. Cross-body coordination and stability.

name: Sciatic Nerve Slider
description: Mobility exercise targeting Sciatic Nerve in the Neural Mobilisation region.
instructions: Seated, extend knee while looking up. Then flex knee while tucking chin.
clinical_notes: Nerve slider technique. Alternating tension points to glide nerve.

name: Sciatic Nerve Tensioner
description: Mobility exercise targeting Sciatic Nerve in the Neural Mobilisation region.
instructions: SLR position with dorsiflexion. Add cervical flexion for upper body tension.
clinical_notes: Tensioning technique. Use only when sliders tolerated. Monitor symptoms.

name: Femoral Nerve Mobilisation
description: Mobility exercise targeting Femoral Nerve in the Neural Mobilisation region.
instructions: Side-lying, flex knee bringing heel toward buttock while extending hip.
clinical_notes: For femoral nerve involvement. L2-L4 radiculopathy management.

name: Brachial Plexus Tension Test Position Exercise
description: Mobility exercise targeting Brachial Plexus in the Neural Mobilisation region.
instructions: ULTT1 position: shoulder abduction, ER, elbow extension, wrist extension. Gentle oscillations.
clinical_notes: Neural mobilisation for upper limb neurodynamics.

name: Slump Stretch (Neural)
description: Mobility exercise targeting Dural Mobility, Sciatic Nerve in the Neural Mobilisation region.
instructions: Seated, slump forward, extend knee, dorsiflex ankle. Release neck to reduce tension.
clinical_notes: Butler neurodynamic technique. Assess vs treat concept.

name: Prone Femoral Nerve Stretch
description: Stretching exercise targeting Femoral Nerve in the Neural Mobilisation region.
instructions: Prone, flex knee. Therapist extends hip while maintaining knee flexion.
clinical_notes: For anterior thigh neural symptoms. Differentiate from quad tightness.

name: Scapular Retraction
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Rhomboids, Middle Trapezius in the Upper Extremity region.
instructions: Squeeze shoulder blades together. Hold 5 seconds.
clinical_notes: Foundation of scapular strengthening.

name: Shoulder Shrug
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Upper Trapezius, Levator Scapulae in the Upper Extremity region.
instructions: Elevate shoulders toward ears. Hold briefly and lower.
clinical_notes: Basic upper trap strengthening.

name: Eccentric Shoulder ER
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Infraspinatus in the Upper Extremity region.
instructions: Side-lying, concentrically raise with other hand, eccentrically lower.
clinical_notes: Eccentric emphasis for rotator cuff tendinopathy.

name: Horizontal Abduction Prone
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Posterior Deltoid, Rhomboids in the Upper Extremity region.
instructions: Prone, arm hanging. Lift arm laterally to horizontal with thumb up.
clinical_notes: Posterior shoulder/scapular strengthening.

name: Bear Hug Exercise
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Subscapularis, Serratus Anterior in the Upper Extremity region.
instructions: Arms in front as if hugging. Push forward with protraction against band.
clinical_notes: Subscapularis and serratus anterior combination.

name: Low Row
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Lower Trapezius, Latissimus Dorsi in the Upper Extremity region.
instructions: Pull band toward hip pockets. Focus on scapular depression.
clinical_notes: Key for lower trap retraining.

name: Lateral Step-Down
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gluteus Medius, Quadriceps in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Stand on step, lower opposite foot to side. Control descent.
clinical_notes: Eccentric glute med and quad. Monitors frontal plane control.

name: Romanian Deadlift (Single Leg)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Hamstrings, Gluteus Maximus in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Single leg RDL with hip hinge. Maintain neutral spine.
clinical_notes: Advanced posterior chain and balance exercise.

name: Box Squat
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Quadriceps, Gluteus Maximus in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Squat to box or chair, pause briefly at bottom.
clinical_notes: Depth control. Reduces fear of falling.

name: Leg Press (Bilateral)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Quadriceps, Gluteus Maximus in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Press platform with both feet. Full ROM controlled.
clinical_notes: Machine-based LE strengthening. Safe for post-op.

name: Standing Calf Raise Machine
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gastrocnemius in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Machine calf raise with full ROM.
clinical_notes: Progressive calf strengthening with measurable load.

name: Seated Hamstring Curl
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Hamstrings in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Machine seated hamstring curl through full ROM.
clinical_notes: Isolated hamstring strengthening. Good for early rehab.

name: Leg Extension Machine
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Quadriceps in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Machine leg extension through full ROM.
clinical_notes: Isolated quad strengthening. Limit ROM if PFJ pain.

name: Isometric Quad Hold at 45°
description: Isometric exercise targeting Quadriceps in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Wall sit or machine hold at 45° knee flexion.
clinical_notes: Isometric strengthening for patellar tendinopathy.

name: Eccentric Single Leg Decline Squat
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Quadriceps, Patellar Tendon in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Single leg squat on 25° decline board. Slow eccentric.
clinical_notes: Visnes protocol for patellar tendinopathy.

name: Adductor Slide
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Adductors in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Standing, slide one foot laterally on slider. Control adduction return.
clinical_notes: Eccentric adductor strengthening. Groin pain prevention.

name: Copenhagen Adductor Exercise
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Adductors in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Side plank with top foot on bench. Lower and raise hips using adductors.
clinical_notes: Strong evidence for groin injury prevention (Harøy 2019).

name: Hamstring Bridge on Ball
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Hamstrings, Core in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Bridge with feet on swiss ball. Curl ball toward buttock.
clinical_notes: Combined hip extension and knee flexion hamstring training.

name: TFL Stretch
