description: Stretching exercise targeting TFL, ITB in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Modified Thomas position with hip in slight adduction.
clinical_notes: Addresses TFL/ITB tightness. Common in runners.

name: IT Band Foam Rolling
description: Mobility exercise targeting ITB, Lateral Thigh in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Side-lying on foam roller from hip to knee along lateral thigh.
clinical_notes: Limited direct ITB evidence but may reduce perceptions of tightness.

name: Hamstring Stretch (Doorway)
description: Stretching exercise targeting Hamstrings in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Lie in doorway, leg up against frame. Adjust distance for intensity.
clinical_notes: Sustained low-load hamstring stretch.

name: Cat-Cow
description: Mobility exercise targeting Spinal Muscles in the Spine region.
instructions: On all fours, alternate between arching and rounding spine.
clinical_notes: General spinal mobility. Warm-up exercise.

name: Child's Pose
description: Stretching exercise targeting Lumbar Extensors, Lats in the Spine region.
instructions: Kneel and sit back on heels. Reach arms forward on floor.
clinical_notes: Gentle lumbar flexion stretch. Rest position between exercises.

name: Cobra Stretch
description: Stretching exercise targeting Abdominals, Hip Flexors in the Spine region.
instructions: Prone, press up on hands with hips on floor. Gentle stretch.
clinical_notes: McKenzie extension. Centralisation principle.

name: Quadruped Hip Circle
description: Mobility exercise targeting Hip Rotators, Core in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: On all fours, make circles with knee maintaining neutral spine.
clinical_notes: Hip mobility with core stability challenge.

name: Supine Glute Stretch
description: Stretching exercise targeting Gluteus Maximus in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Supine, pull knee across body toward opposite shoulder.
clinical_notes: Gluteal and piriformis stretch variant.

name: Prone Extension (Swan)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Erector Spinae, Multifidus in the Spine region.
instructions: Prone, lift chest off surface using back extensors. Arms at sides.
clinical_notes: Back extensor strengthening. Monitor for lumbar hyperextension.

name: Seated Lumbar Flexion Stretch
description: Stretching exercise targeting Lumbar Extensors in the Spine region.
instructions: Seated, slump forward reaching toward floor between knees.
clinical_notes: Williams flexion-based stretch. Useful for stenosis.

name: Prayer Stretch
description: Stretching exercise targeting Lumbar Extensors, Thoracolumbar Fascia in the Spine region.
instructions: Kneeling, reach arms forward while sitting back on heels.
clinical_notes: Lumbosacral stretch variant.

name: Sidelying Thoracic Rotation
description: Mobility exercise targeting Thoracic Rotators in the Spine region.
instructions: Side-lying, bottom leg straight, top knee on roller. Rotate upper body.
clinical_notes: Thoracic rotation with lower body stabilised.

name: Standing Back Extension
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Erector Spinae in the Spine region.
instructions: Hands on low back, lean backward gently.
clinical_notes: Standing extension mobilisation. McKenzie standing extension.

name: Single Leg Balance Eyes Closed
description: Balance exercise targeting Ankle Stabilisers, Core in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Stand on one leg with eyes closed. Maintain balance.
clinical_notes: Advanced proprioception. Removes visual input.

name: Star Excursion Balance Test (Y-Balance)
description: Balance exercise targeting Lower Extremity in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Single leg, reach in anterior, posterolateral, posteromedial directions.
clinical_notes: Both test and training exercise. Identifies deficits.

name: Lateral Bound
description: Plyometric exercise targeting Gluteus Medius, Quadriceps in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Single leg lateral jump, land on opposite leg. Control landing.
clinical_notes: Frontal plane power. Return-to-sport progression.

name: Depth Jump
description: Plyometric exercise targeting Quadriceps, Posterior Chain in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Step off higher box, land, immediately jump to second box.
clinical_notes: Advanced reactive strength training.

name: Split Jump
description: Plyometric exercise targeting Quadriceps, Gluteus Maximus in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Lunge position, jump and switch legs mid-air.
clinical_notes: Dynamic lunge pattern. Monitor landing alignment.

name: Single Leg Hop
description: Plyometric exercise targeting Quadriceps, Calf Complex in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Single leg forward hop for distance.
clinical_notes: Return-to-sport test and training. >90% limb symmetry index.

name: Triple Hop Test
description: Plyometric exercise targeting Lower Extremity in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Three consecutive single-leg hops for distance.
clinical_notes: Functional outcome measure and advanced training.

name: Crossover Hop
description: Plyometric exercise targeting Lower Extremity, Hip Stabilisers in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Single-leg hop crossing over a line with each hop.
clinical_notes: Multi-planar hop test. Tests frontal plane control.

name: Wrist Flexion Stretch
description: Stretching exercise targeting Wrist Flexors in the Upper Extremity region.
instructions: Extend arm, palm up, pull fingers back with other hand.
clinical_notes: Common for medial epicondylalgia.

name: Wrist Extension Stretch
description: Stretching exercise targeting Wrist Extensors in the Upper Extremity region.
instructions: Extend arm, palm down, pull hand down with other hand.
clinical_notes: Common for lateral epicondylalgia.

name: Forearm Pronation Strengthening
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Pronator Teres in the Upper Extremity region.
instructions: Hold hammer, rotate forearm to pronation against gravity.
clinical_notes: Progressive forearm strengthening.

name: Finger Abduction Exercise
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Hand Intrinsics in the Upper Extremity region.
instructions: Spread fingers apart against rubber band resistance.
clinical_notes: Intrinsic hand muscle strengthening.

name: Hook Grip Exercise
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Finger Flexors in the Upper Extremity region.
instructions: Grip bar with fingers only (hook grip). Hold.
clinical_notes: Grip endurance training.

name: SIJ Stabilisation (Pelvic Belt)
description: Isometric exercise targeting Core, Pelvic Floor in the Spine region.
instructions: With pelvic belt, perform TA activation and pelvic floor contraction.
clinical_notes: SIJ stabilisation with external compression assistance.

name: SIJ Muscle Energy Technique (Self)
description: Mobility exercise targeting Hip Flexors, Piriformis in the Spine region.
instructions: Supine, push knee against hand in specific directions for 5 seconds. Reposition.
clinical_notes: Self-MET for SIJ dysfunction. Direction depends on dysfunction.

name: Squat to Press
description: Functional exercise targeting Quadriceps, Shoulders in the Full Body region.
instructions: Squat holding weights at shoulders. Stand and press overhead.
clinical_notes: Integrated lower and upper body functional exercise.

name: Medicine Ball Rotation Throw
description: Functional exercise targeting Obliques, Core in the Core region.
instructions: Stand sideways to wall, rotate and throw medicine ball.
clinical_notes: Rotational power training. Sport-specific.

name: Battle Rope Waves
description: Functional exercise targeting Shoulders, Core in the Full Body region.
instructions: Create alternating waves with battle ropes.
clinical_notes: Shoulder endurance and core stabilisation.

name: TRX Row
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Rhomboids, Lats in the Upper Extremity region.
instructions: Lean back holding TRX, pull chest to hands.
clinical_notes: Adjustable difficulty pulling exercise.

name: TRX Squat
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Quadriceps, Gluteus Maximus in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Hold TRX handles, squat back with assisted balance.
clinical_notes: Supported squat for early rehab.

name: TRX Push-Up
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Pectorals, Core in the Upper Extremity region.
instructions: Hands in TRX straps, perform push-up on unstable surface.
clinical_notes: Unstable surface push-up. Increased core demand.

name: Cable Woodchop
description: Functional exercise targeting Obliques, Core in the Core region.
instructions: High to low diagonal pull across body.
clinical_notes: Functional rotation pattern. Anti-rotation demand.

name: Reverse Lunge
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Quadriceps, Gluteus Maximus in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Step backward into lunge position. Control descent.
clinical_notes: Less anterior knee stress than forward lunge.

name: Lateral Lunge
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Adductors, Quadriceps in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Step laterally into wide squat on one leg.
clinical_notes: Frontal plane strengthening. Addresses adductor flexibility.

name: Curtsy Lunge
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gluteus Medius, Quadriceps in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Step behind and across other leg into curtsy. Return.
clinical_notes: Multi-planar hip and knee strengthening.

name: Walking Lunge
description: Functional exercise targeting Quadriceps, Gluteus Maximus in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Continuous alternating forward lunges while walking.
clinical_notes: Dynamic functional lower extremity exercise.

name: Step Over (Agility)
description: Functional exercise targeting Hip Flexors, Core in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Step over hurdles alternating lead leg. Maintain upright posture.
clinical_notes: Hip mobility with dynamic balance.

name: Resistance Band Walk (Forward)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Hip Flexors, Gluteus Medius in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Band around ankles, walk forward maintaining squat position.
clinical_notes: Multi-directional hip strengthening.

name: Soleus Stretch (Wall)
description: Stretching exercise targeting Soleus in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Wall stretch with knee bent to target soleus.
clinical_notes: Knee flexed to bypass gastrocnemius.

name: Seated Calf Raise
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Soleus in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Seated with weight on knees, raise heels.
clinical_notes: Isolated soleus strengthening.

name: Ankle Plantarflexion Band
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gastrocnemius, Soleus in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Sit with band around forefoot. Push foot down against resistance.
clinical_notes: Resisted plantarflexion exercise.

name: Toe Raises
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Tibialis Anterior in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Stand, lift toes off ground keeping heels planted.
clinical_notes: Tibialis anterior strengthening. Useful for foot drop rehab.

name: Towel Scrunch
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Intrinsic Foot Muscles in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Place towel on floor, scrunch toward you using toes.
clinical_notes: Intrinsic foot strengthening. Common for plantar fasciitis.

name: Marble Pick-Up
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Intrinsic Foot Muscles in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Pick up marbles with toes, place in cup.
clinical_notes: Fine motor foot exercise. Toe dexterity and intrinsic strength.

name: Standing Gastrocnemius Raise (Eccentric)
description: Strengthening exercise targeting Gastrocnemius in the Lower Extremity region.
instructions: Rise on both feet, lower on single leg slowly.
clinical_notes: Eccentric calf protocol for Achilles tendinopathy.

name: Cervical Extension AROM
description: Mobility exercise for Cervical Spine.
instructions: Slowly extend neck looking upward. Return to neutral.

name: Cervical Protraction/Retraction
description: Mobility exercise for Cervical Spine.
instructions: Glide chin forward then retract. Rhythmic motion.

name: Cervical SNAG C5-C6
description: Mobility exercise for Cervical Spine.
instructions: Towel-assisted sustained natural apophyseal glide at C5-6.

name: DNF Training with Pressure Biofeedback
description: Isometric exercise for Cervical Spine.
instructions: Supine with pressure biofeedback under occiput. Perform cranio-cervical flexion to target pressures.

name: Cervical Stabilisation with Arm Movement
description: Functional exercise for Cervical Spine.
instructions: Maintain chin tuck while performing arm movements.

name: Empty Can Exercise
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Raise arm in scapular plane with thumb down to shoulder height.

name: ER at 0° Abduction
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Elbow at side, rotate forearm outward against band.

name: Supine Flexion with Dowel
description: Mobility exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Supine, use dowel to assist shoulder flexion with uninvolved arm.

name: Behind-the-Back IR Stretch
description: Stretching exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Reach behind back with towel. Pull upward with top hand.

name: Shoulder Abduction Isometric
description: Isometric exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Push arm outward against wall without movement.

name: Lat Stretch (Doorway)
description: Stretching exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Hold door frame overhead, lean away stretching lat.

name: 90/90 Hip Lift
description: Isometric exercise for Spine.
instructions: Supine, feet on wall at 90° hip/knee. Lift tailbone slightly.

name: Prone Hip Extension (Gluteal)
description: Strengthening exercise for Spine.
instructions: Prone, extend hip keeping knee bent. Focus on glute activation.

name: Windshield Wipers
description: Mobility exercise for Spine.
instructions: Supine, knees bent, drop knees side to side.

name: Half Kneeling Hip Flexor with Rotation
description: Stretching exercise for Spine.
instructions: Half kneeling hip flexor stretch adding thoracic rotation.

name: Eccentric Step-Down (Slow)
description: Strengthening exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Step down over 4 seconds. Control eccentric.

name: Hamstring Walkout
description: Strengthening exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Bridge position, walk feet out and back maintaining hips.

name: Heel Walk
description: Strengthening exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Walk on heels with toes lifted.

name: Toe Walk
description: Strengthening exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Walk on toes.

name: Lateral Ankle Inversion Stretch
description: Stretching exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Sit, gently invert ankle to stretch peroneals.

name: Median Nerve Slider (Full)
description: Mobility exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: ULTT1 position with alternating wrist/neck movements.

name: Radial Nerve Slider
description: Mobility exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Depression, IR, pronation, wrist flexion with alternating components.

name: Ulnar Nerve Slider
description: Mobility exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: ULTT3 position with alternating elbow/wrist movements.

name: BOSU Single Leg Squat
description: Balance exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Single leg squat on BOSU ball.

name: Tandem Walk
description: Balance exercise for Full Body.
instructions: Walk heel-to-toe in straight line.

name: Wobble Board Multi-Direction
description: Balance exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Stand on wobble board, maintain balance in all planes.

name: Ab Wheel Rollout
description: Strengthening exercise for Core.
instructions: Kneeling ab wheel rollout. Maintain neutral spine.

name: Reverse Plank
description: Isometric exercise for Core.
instructions: Support body face-up on hands and heels.

name: Side Plank with Hip Abduction
description: Strengthening exercise for Core.
instructions: Side plank lifting top leg.

name: Bicycle Crunch
description: Strengthening exercise for Core.
instructions: Alternate elbow to opposite knee while cycling legs.

name: V-Sit Hold
description: Isometric exercise for Core.
instructions: Balance on sit bones with legs and torso elevated.

name: Plank with Arm Reach
description: Strengthening exercise for Core.
instructions: Plank position, alternate reaching arms forward.

name: Mountain Climbers (Controlled)
description: Functional exercise for Core.
instructions: Plank position, alternate bringing knees to chest slowly.

name: Overhead Squat
description: Functional exercise for Full Body.
instructions: Squat with arms overhead holding dowel. Maintain posture.

name: Single Arm Farmer's Carry
description: Functional exercise for Full Body.
instructions: Walk with weight in one hand.

name: Renegade Row
description: Strengthening exercise for Full Body.
instructions: Plank on dumbbells, alternate rowing.

name: Landmine Press
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Press barbell in landmine attachment overhead.

name: Band Pull-Apart
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Hold band at chest, pull apart horizontally.

name: Wall Slide with Band
description: Functional exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Wall angel with light band around wrists for resistance.

name: Scapular Push-Up
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Push-up position, protract and retract scapulae without bending elbows.

name: Shoulder Tap Plank
description: Functional exercise for Core.
instructions: Plank position, alternate tapping opposite shoulder.

name: Pec Major Stretch (Corner)
description: Stretching exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Arms on corner walls at 90°, lean through.

name: Prone Quad Stretch
description: Stretching exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Prone, pull heel toward buttock with hand or strap.

name: Seated Groin Stretch (Butterfly)
description: Stretching exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Sit with soles together, gently press knees down.

name: Standing Adductor Stretch
description: Stretching exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Wide stance, shift weight to one side.

name: Figure-4 Glute Stretch
description: Stretching exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Supine, ankle on opposite knee, pull toward chest.

name: Standing Hamstring Stretch
description: Stretching exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Foot on elevated surface, hinge forward at hips.

name: 90/90 Hip Stretch
description: Stretching exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Sit with front and back leg at 90°. Lean forward.

name: Isometric Hip Adduction (Ball Squeeze)
description: Isometric exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Squeeze ball between knees in various positions.

name: Eccentric Tibialis Posterior
description: Strengthening exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Single leg heel raise emphasising inversion. Slow eccentric.

name: Arch Doming
description: Strengthening exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Pull ball of foot toward heel without curling toes.

name: Cervical Retraction with Overpressure
description: Mobility exercise for Cervical Spine.
instructions: Chin tuck with additional hand pressure at end range.

name: Upper Cervical Flexion (Nod)
description: Isometric exercise for Cervical Spine.
instructions: Gentle nodding motion activating deep flexors. Minimal range.

name: Cervical Retraction Walking
description: Functional exercise for Cervical Spine.
instructions: Maintain chin tuck while walking. Postural retraining.

name: Eccentric Wrist Radial Deviation
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Hammer eccentric radial deviation. Slow controlled.

name: Isometric Wrist Extension
description: Isometric exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Push back of hand against immovable object. Hold.

name: Eccentric Hip Adduction (Side-Lying)
description: Strengthening exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Side-lying, slowly lower top leg from abducted position.

name: Prone Knee Flexion (Hamstring)
description: Strengthening exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Prone, flex knee against gravity. Add ankle weight.

name: Hip Internal Rotation Stretch
description: Stretching exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Prone or seated, allow knees to fall inward.

name: Hip External Rotation Stretch
description: Stretching exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Seated, cross ankle on knee, lean forward.

name: Theraband Shoulder Flexion
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Stand on band, raise arm forward to 90°.

name: Theraband Shoulder Extension
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Band attached in front, pull arm backward.

name: Theraband Shoulder Abduction
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Stand on band, raise arm to side to 90°.

name: Resisted Cervical Rotation
description: Strengthening exercise for Cervical Spine.
instructions: Band around head, rotate against resistance.

name: Chin Tuck with Cervical Rotation
description: Strengthening exercise for Cervical Spine.
instructions: Maintain chin tuck while adding rotation.

name: Perturbation Training (Standing)
description: Balance exercise for Full Body.
instructions: Partner applies unexpected pushes while patient maintains balance.

name: Single Leg Stance on Foam
description: Balance exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Stand on one leg on foam pad.

name: Tandem Stance
description: Balance exercise for Full Body.
instructions: Stand heel-to-toe. Hold position.

name: Four-Point Kneeling Weight Shift
description: Balance exercise for Core.
instructions: On all fours, shift weight in all directions.

name: Swiss Ball Sitting Balance
description: Balance exercise for Core.
instructions: Sit on swiss ball maintaining balance. Add arm movements.

name: Reactive Single Leg Landing
description: Plyometric exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Jump and land on single leg with unexpected direction change.

name: Lateral Shuffle
description: Functional exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Quick lateral shuffling steps.

name: Carioca
description: Functional exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Lateral movement with crossover steps.

name: Backward Walking
description: Functional exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Walk backward with controlled steps.

name: Single Leg Hop and Hold
description: Plyometric exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Hop forward on single leg and stick the landing.

name: TMJ Opening Exercise
description: Mobility exercise for Cervical Spine.
instructions: Controlled jaw opening maintaining midline.

name: TMJ Lateral Deviation Exercise
description: Mobility exercise for Cervical Spine.
instructions: Shift jaw side to side in controlled manner.

name: Thoracic Extension on Chair
description: Mobility exercise for Spine.
instructions: Sit, interlace hands behind head, extend over chair back.

name: Quadruped Thoracic Rotation
description: Mobility exercise for Spine.
instructions: On all fours, hand behind head, rotate thorax upward.

name: Supine Thoracic Extension
description: Mobility exercise for Spine.
instructions: Supine on foam roller along spine. Open arms.

name: Eccentric Calf Lower (Straight Knee)
description: Strengthening exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Rise bilateral, lower single leg with straight knee. Step edge.

name: Eccentric Calf Lower (Bent Knee)
description: Strengthening exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Rise bilateral, lower single leg with knee slightly bent.

name: Heavy Slow Resistance Heel Raise
description: Strengthening exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Seated and standing calf raises 6RM progressing to 8RM.

name: Isometric Midrange Calf Hold
description: Isometric exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Hold calf raise at mid-range for pain relief.

name: Prone Horizontal Abduction
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Prone, lift arm to side at 90° with thumb up.

name: Dynamic Hug
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Band around back, push forward in hugging motion with protraction.

name: Shoulder ER in Throwing Position
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: 90/90 position, eccentrically control IR.

name: Shoulder Isometric Adduction
description: Isometric exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Squeeze pillow between arm and body.

name: Resisted Shoulder Protraction
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Push forward against band at 90° flexion.

name: Supine Shoulder Flexion (Active Assisted)
description: Mobility exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Use wand to assist flexion ROM. Uninvolved arm helps.

name: Sidelying ER (Pillow Under Arm)
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Side-lying ER with pillow support under arm for comfort.

name: Standing IR Behind Back
description: Stretching exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Reach behind back, use towel to assist.

name: Shoulder Proprioception (Ball on Wall)
description: Balance exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Press ball against wall with outstretched arm. Make circles.

name: Elbow Flexion Curl
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Standard bicep curl with dumbbell.

name: Elbow Extension (Overhead)
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Overhead tricep extension with dumbbell.

name: Wrist Circle
description: Mobility exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Make circles with wrist in both directions.

name: Finger Flexor Stretch
description: Stretching exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Extend fingers and wrist back gently.

name: Hip Circles (Standing)
description: Mobility exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Standing, make large circles with hip.

name: Banded Hip Flexion March
description: Strengthening exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Standing with band around feet, march in place.

name: Supine Hip IR/ER
description: Mobility exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Supine, feet flat, drop knees in and out.

name: Gastrocnemius Eccentric Loading
description: Strengthening exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Eccentric calf lower with straight knee, slow 3-sec count.

name: Side Step-Up
description: Strengthening exercise for Lower Extremity.
instructions: Step up laterally onto box. Control descent.

name: Cross-Body March
description: Functional exercise for Core.
instructions: Standing, bring knee toward opposite elbow.

name: Plank to Push-Up
description: Strengthening exercise for Core.
instructions: Alternate between forearm and hand plank.

name: Russian Twist
description: Strengthening exercise for Core.
instructions: Seated lean back, rotate torso with or without weight.

name: Prone Plank on Elbows
description: Isometric exercise for Core.
instructions: Standard forearm plank. Maintain neutral spine.

name: Dead Hang
description: Stretching exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Hang from pull-up bar with full bodyweight.

name: Scapular Depression Exercise
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Seated, press hands into surface and depress scapulae.

name: Scapular Elevation (Shrug)
description: Strengthening exercise for Upper Extremity.
instructions: Elevate shoulders toward ears with weights.

name: Back Extension (Prone)
description: Prone back extension for erector spinae strengthening.
instructions: Lie prone with hands behind head. Lift chest off floor by extending spine. Hold 2-3 seconds at top.
clinical_notes: Fundamental spinal extensor exercise. Avoid hyperextension.

name: Bird Dog
description: Quadruped opposite arm/leg raise for spinal stability.
instructions: In quadruped, extend opposite arm and leg simultaneously. Maintain neutral spine. Hold 5 seconds.
clinical_notes: McGill Big 3 exercise. Excellent for spinal stabilisation.

name: Superman Hold
description: Full body extension hold for posterior chain endurance.
instructions: Lie prone, simultaneously lift arms, chest and legs off floor. Hold position.
clinical_notes: High spinal extensor activation. Contraindicated in acute disc herniation.

name: Deadlift (Conventional)
description: Compound posterior chain exercise with heavy loading.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width, grip bar outside knees. Drive through heels, extend hips and knees simultaneously maintaining neutral spine.
clinical_notes: High spinal loading. Ensure proper technique. Contraindicated in acute LBP.

name: Good Morning
description: Hip hinge pattern for erector spinae and hamstring loading.
instructions: Bar across upper back, hinge at hips keeping spine neutral. Lower torso to ~45 degrees, drive hips forward to return.
clinical_notes: Excellent posterior chain exercise. Light loads initially.

name: Cat-Cow Stretch
description: Spinal flexion/extension mobility for erector spinae.
instructions: In quadruped, alternate between arching back (cow) and rounding spine (cat). Move through full pain-free range.
clinical_notes: Excellent warm-up and pain relief for spinal stiffness.

name: Prone Cobra
description: Sustained prone extension with external rotation for thoracic extensors.
instructions: Lie prone, lift chest with arms at sides, externally rotate arms with thumbs up. Hold position.
clinical_notes: Great for upper back and postural muscles.

name: Reverse Hyper Extension
description: Hip extension with spinal decompression effect.
instructions: Lie prone on elevated surface, legs hanging. Extend hips lifting legs to horizontal. Control descent.
clinical_notes: Decompression effect beneficial for disc pathology.

name: Seated Thoracic Extension
description: Thoracic spine extension mobilisation over foam roller.
instructions: Sit or lie with foam roller at mid-thoracic level. Extend over roller with arms crossed. Segmentally move roller.
clinical_notes: Key for thoracic kyphosis and shoulder impingement patients.

name: Side Bridge (McGill)
description: Lateral spinal stabilisation exercise from McGill Big 3.
instructions: Side-lying on elbow, lift hips off floor maintaining straight line. Hold position.
clinical_notes: McGill Big 3. Excellent lateral stabiliser with low spinal compression.

name: Side-Lying QL Stretch
description: Lateral stretch targeting the quadratus lumborum.
instructions: Lie on side, extend top arm overhead and cross bottom leg over top. Reach arm away to elongate lateral trunk.
clinical_notes: Excellent for lateral low back pain and QL trigger points.

name: Standing QL Side Bend
description: Active side bending stretch for quadratus lumborum.
instructions: Stand feet shoulder-width, reach one arm overhead and lean to opposite side. Maintain frontal plane movement.
clinical_notes: Good for desk workers with lateral trunk stiffness.

name: Suitcase Carry
description: Unilateral loaded carry for QL and core anti-lateral flexion.
instructions: Hold heavy dumbbell/kettlebell in one hand at side. Walk maintaining upright posture without leaning.
clinical_notes: Anti-lateral flexion exercise. Key for lumbar stabilisation and QL strengthening.

name: Side Plank with Hip Drop
description: Dynamic side plank targeting QL and lateral core.
instructions: From side plank position, drop hips toward floor then lift back to neutral. Maintain alignment.
clinical_notes: Excellent QL and oblique co-activation. Progress from bent knee to full side plank.

name: QL Release (Tennis Ball)
description: Self-myofascial release for quadratus lumborum trigger points.
instructions: Place tennis ball between rib cage and iliac crest posterolaterally. Lie on ball and apply gentle pressure, rolling slowly.
clinical_notes: Common trigger point referral to SI joint and hip. Use caution near kidney area.

name: Chest Fly (Dumbbell)
description: Pectoralis major isolation in horizontal plane.
instructions: Lie supine on bench, hold dumbbells above chest. Open arms wide with slight elbow bend, return to start.
clinical_notes: Excellent for pec strengthening post-surgery. Control ROM to avoid anterior capsule stress.

name: Pec Minor Stretch (Doorway)
description: Corner or doorway stretch for pectoralis minor and anterior chest.
instructions: Stand in doorway, forearms on frame at 90°. Step through gently until stretch is felt in anterior chest.
clinical_notes: Essential for forward head posture and scapular dyskinesis. Key in shoulder impingement rehab.

name: Lat Pulldown
description: Vertical pulling exercise for latissimus dorsi.
instructions: Grip bar wider than shoulder-width. Pull bar to upper chest, squeezing shoulder blades together.
clinical_notes: Foundational back exercise. Avoid behind-the-neck variation.

name: Straight Arm Lat Pulldown
description: Isolated latissimus dorsi activation in shoulder extension.
instructions: Stand facing cable, arms extended. Pull bar down to thighs keeping arms straight.
clinical_notes: Excellent lat isolation without biceps compensation.

name: Levator Scapulae Stretch
description: Targeted stretch for levator scapulae and upper cervical tension.
instructions: Sit tall, rotate head 45° to one side, tuck chin and look toward armpit. Gently pull head with hand.
clinical_notes: Key stretch for neck pain and tension headaches. Avoid overpressure.

name: Bench Press (Flat)
description: Compound pressing movement for pectoralis major, anterior deltoid and triceps.
instructions: Lie on bench, grip bar slightly wider than shoulders. Lower to chest, press up maintaining scapular retraction.
clinical_notes: Ensure scapular retraction throughout. Avoid excessive shoulder extension at bottom.

name: Bent Over Row
description: Compound horizontal pulling for latissimus dorsi and posterior chain.
instructions: Hip hinge position, pull barbell/dumbbells to lower chest. Squeeze shoulder blades at top.
clinical_notes: Key for posterior chain and postural correction. Maintain neutral spine.

name: Quadruped QL Side Lift
description: Quadruped hip hike for isolated QL activation.
instructions: In quadruped, maintain spine neutral. Hike one hip upward (lateral pelvic tilt) using QL. Control return.
clinical_notes: Isolated QL activation. Useful for asymmetric weakness assessment.

name: Sartorius Stretch (Modified Lunge)
description: Stretch targeting sartorius in combined hip extension and knee flexion.
instructions: Half-kneeling lunge, reach back to grab rear ankle pulling heel to buttock. Push hips forward gently.
clinical_notes: Targets sartorius which crosses both hip and knee. Useful for pes anserinus issues.

name: Goblet Squat
description: Front-loaded squat for quad and glute development.
instructions: Hold kettlebell at chest, squat to parallel keeping torso upright.
clinical_notes: Excellent for teaching squat pattern.

name: Barbell Back Squat
description: Fundamental compound lower body exercise.
instructions: Bar on upper traps, squat to parallel or below. Drive through heels.
clinical_notes: King of lower body exercises. Ensure adequate ankle dorsiflexion.

name: Front Squat
description: Anterior-loaded squat emphasizing quads and upright posture.
instructions: Bar on front delts, elbows high, squat deep with upright torso.
clinical_notes: Requires good thoracic and wrist mobility.

name: Leg Extension Machine
description: Isolated quadriceps strengthening.
instructions: Seated, extend knee against pad through full range.
clinical_notes: Open kinetic chain. Use cautiously with ACL rehab patients.

name: Leg Curl Machine (Seated)
description: Seated hamstring curl for isolated hamstring loading.
instructions: Seated, curl heels toward buttocks against resistance.
clinical_notes: Good for hamstring isolation in early rehab phases.

name: Sumo Deadlift
description: Wide-stance deadlift emphasizing adductors and glutes.
instructions: Wide stance, grip inside knees, drive hips through to lockout.
clinical_notes: Less spinal loading than conventional deadlift.

name: Pistol Squat
description: Single-leg full depth squat for advanced lower body control.
instructions: Stand on one leg, squat to full depth with other leg extended. Stand back up.
clinical_notes: Requires excellent mobility, strength, and balance.

name: Walking Lunge
description: Dynamic lunge for quad, glute, and balance training.
instructions: Step forward into lunge, push off to next step. Alternate legs.
clinical_notes: Good functional exercise. Watch for valgus collapse.

name: Reverse Lunge
description: Posterior-stepping lunge reducing knee shear forces.
instructions: Step backward into lunge, lower until both knees at 90°. Return to standing.
clinical_notes: Safer for knees than forward lunges. Good for patellofemoral patients.

name: Step-Up (Forward)
description: Forward step-up for unilateral quad and glute strength.
instructions: Step onto box with lead leg, fully extend hip at top. Step down controlled.
clinical_notes: Adjust box height for difficulty. 6-12 inches typical.

name: Sissy Squat
description: Knee-dominant squat for quad isolation.
instructions: Hold support, lean back while bending knees forward. Lower until stretch felt.
clinical_notes: High patellofemoral load. Avoid with PFJ pain.

name: Donkey Kick
description: Hip extension exercise targeting gluteus maximus.
instructions: On all fours, kick one leg back and up keeping knee bent. Squeeze glute at top.
clinical_notes: Ensure movement comes from hip, not lumbar extension.

name: Fire Hydrant
description: Hip abduction in quadruped for glute medius activation.
instructions: On all fours, lift knee out to side keeping 90° bend. Control return.
clinical_notes: Good activation drill before hip-dominant exercises.

name: Standing Hip Abduction (Cable)
description: Standing hip abduction against cable resistance.
instructions: Cable at ankle, stand on one leg, abduct other leg against resistance.
clinical_notes: Functional standing position for hip abductor training.

name: Seated Hip Adduction Machine
description: Machine-based hip adductor strengthening.
instructions: Seated, squeeze legs together against pads through full range.
clinical_notes: For groin strengthening. Start light after groin injuries.

name: Tibialis Anterior Raise
description: Dorsiflexion strengthening for shin splint prevention.
instructions: Lean back against wall, lift toes toward shins. Lower controlled.
clinical_notes: Essential for medial tibial stress syndrome prevention.

name: Toe Curls (Towel Scrunches)
description: Intrinsic foot muscle strengthening.
instructions: Seated, scrunch towel with toes. Pull towel toward you.
clinical_notes: For plantar fasciitis and flat foot management.

name: Heel Walk
description: Walking on heels for tibialis anterior and ankle dorsiflexor strength.
instructions: Walk on heels keeping toes off ground for set distance.
clinical_notes: Simple assessment and treatment for ankle dorsiflexor weakness.

name: Bench Press
description: Fundamental horizontal pushing exercise for chest and triceps.
instructions: Lie on bench, lower bar to chest, press to lockout. Retract scapulae.
clinical_notes: Ensure scapular retraction to protect shoulders.

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
description: Upper chest and anterior deltoid pressing.
instructions: 30-45° incline bench, press dumbbells up and slightly together.
clinical_notes: Good for upper chest emphasis. Avoid excessive incline.

name: Dumbbell Fly
description: Chest isolation through horizontal adduction.
instructions: Flat bench, arms extended, lower dumbbells in arc to stretch position. Return.
clinical_notes: Use light weight initially. Avoid excessive shoulder extension.

name: Overhead Press (Barbell)
description: Standing barbell press for shoulder and upper body strength.
instructions: Bar at shoulders, press overhead to lockout. Avoid excessive lumbar extension.
clinical_notes: Requires good thoracic extension and shoulder mobility.

name: Lateral Raise
description: Isolated lateral deltoid strengthening.
instructions: Arms at sides, raise dumbbells laterally to shoulder height. Control descent.
clinical_notes: Keep thumbs slightly up to avoid impingement. Below 90° for subacromial issues.

name: Front Raise
description: Anterior deltoid isolation exercise.
instructions: Raise dumbbells forward to shoulder height with straight or slightly bent arms.
clinical_notes: Often unnecessary as anterior deltoid is trained in all pressing.

name: Reverse Fly (Prone)
description: Posterior deltoid and scapular retractor strengthening.
instructions: Prone on incline bench, raise dumbbells laterally squeezing shoulder blades.
clinical_notes: Excellent for postural correction and scapular stability.

name: Face Pull
description: Cable external rotation and scapular retraction exercise.
instructions: High cable with rope, pull toward face separating hands. Squeeze retractors.
clinical_notes: Essential shoulder prehab exercise. Counteracts pressing dominance.

name: Barbell Curl
description: Bilateral biceps strengthening.
instructions: Stand with bar at thigh, curl to shoulders keeping elbows at sides.
clinical_notes: Avoid swinging. Useful for distal biceps tendon loading.

name: Hammer Curl
description: Neutral grip curl for brachialis and brachioradialis.
instructions: Neutral grip dumbbells, curl to shoulders keeping thumbs up.
clinical_notes: Less stress on biceps tendon than supinated curls.

name: Triceps Pushdown
description: Cable triceps extension for triceps isolation.
instructions: Cable with bar/rope, extend elbows pushing down. Keep elbows at sides.
clinical_notes: Good isolation exercise for triceps.

name: Overhead Triceps Extension
description: Triceps long head emphasis with overhead position.
instructions: Dumbbell overhead, lower behind head bending elbow. Extend back up.
clinical_notes: Long head gets best stretch in overhead position.

name: Skull Crusher
description: Lying triceps extension for mass building.
instructions: Lying on bench, lower barbell/dumbbells toward forehead. Extend elbows.
clinical_notes: Can aggravate elbow issues. Use EZ bar to reduce wrist strain.

name: Bent Over Barbell Row
description: Horizontal pull for back thickness and posture.
instructions: Hip hinge, pull barbell to lower chest/upper abdomen. Squeeze scapulae.
clinical_notes: Maintain neutral spine throughout. Core engagement critical.

name: Seated Cable Row
description: Horizontal rowing for mid-back and scapular retractors.
instructions: Seated, pull handle to abdomen squeezing shoulder blades. Control return.
clinical_notes: Excellent for postural muscles. Cue scapular retraction.

name: Single Arm Dumbbell Row
description: Unilateral horizontal row for lat and scapular muscles.
instructions: One hand/knee on bench, row dumbbell to hip. Squeeze at top.
clinical_notes: Allows unilateral correction of imbalances.

name: Pull-Up
description: Bodyweight vertical pull for lat and upper back development.
instructions: Overhand grip, pull chin above bar. Lower under control.
clinical_notes: Foundational pulling exercise. Assist with band if needed.

name: Chin-Up
description: Supinated grip pull-up for biceps and lat emphasis.
instructions: Underhand grip, pull chin above bar. Lower controlled.
clinical_notes: Higher biceps activation than pull-up.

name: Lat Pulldown
description: Machine vertical pull for lat development.
instructions: Wide grip, pull bar to upper chest. Control return.
clinical_notes: Good pull-up alternative. Avoid behind-neck variation.

name: Cable Woodchop
description: Rotational core and oblique exercise with cable.
instructions: High-to-low or low-to-high cable rotation. Pivot feet, rotate through hips.
clinical_notes: Functional rotational training. Progress load gradually.

name: Wrist Curl (Barbell)
description: Forearm flexor strengthening.
instructions: Forearms on bench, palms up, curl barbell with wrists.
clinical_notes: For medial epicondylalgia and grip strength.

name: Reverse Wrist Curl
description: Forearm extensor strengthening.
instructions: Forearms on bench, palms down, extend wrist lifting barbell.
clinical_notes: Essential for lateral epicondylalgia (tennis elbow).

name: Farmer's Walk
description: Loaded carry for grip, core, and total body conditioning.
instructions: Hold heavy dumbbells/kettlebells at sides, walk maintaining posture.
clinical_notes: Excellent functional exercise. Builds grip endurance.

name: Turkish Get-Up
description: Full body mobility and stability exercise with kettlebell.
instructions: From supine with KB overhead, move through 7 positions to standing.
clinical_notes: Assess and train whole-body stability and mobility.

name: Kettlebell Swing
description: Explosive hip hinge for posterior chain power.
instructions: Hip hinge, swing KB between legs, drive hips forward to chest height.
clinical_notes: Teaches hip hinge pattern. Excellent for low back patients.

name: Medicine Ball Slam
description: Explosive overhead throw for power and conditioning.
instructions: Raise ball overhead, slam forcefully to ground. Catch bounce.
clinical_notes: Good for power development and stress relief.

name: Plank
description: Fundamental isometric core stability exercise.
instructions: Forearms on ground, body straight from head to heels. Hold position.
clinical_notes: Foundation core exercise. Avoid lumbar sag.

name: Side Plank
description: Lateral core stability for obliques and hip.
instructions: Forearm on ground, stack feet, lift hips. Body straight.
clinical_notes: Excellent for scoliosis and lateral core weakness.

name: Dead Bug
description: Anti-extension core exercise for motor control.
instructions: Supine, arms up, knees at 90°. Extend opposite arm/leg maintaining flat back.
clinical_notes: Teaches core bracing with limb movement. Fundamental motor control.

name: Bird Dog
description: Anti-rotation and extension core stability.
instructions: Quadruped, extend opposite arm and leg. Hold briefly. Return.
clinical_notes: McGill Big 3 exercise. Safe for acute LBP.

name: Ab Wheel Rollout
description: Advanced anti-extension core exercise.
instructions: Kneel, roll wheel forward extending body. Pull back to start.
clinical_notes: Very high core demand. Start from knees, progress to standing.

name: Hanging Leg Raise
description: Advanced hip flexion and lower abdominal exercise.
instructions: Hang from bar, raise legs to parallel or above. Lower controlled.
clinical_notes: Requires grip strength. Bend knees to regress.

name: Russian Twist
description: Rotational core exercise for obliques.
instructions: Seated, lean back 45°, rotate torso side to side with or without weight.
clinical_notes: Avoid with disc pathology. Control movement speed.

name: Pallof Press
description: Anti-rotation core exercise with cable/band.
instructions: Cable at chest height, press hands forward resisting rotation. Hold.
clinical_notes: Excellent anti-rotation training. Safe for most patients.

name: Conventional Deadlift
description: Fundamental hip hinge for posterior chain development.
instructions: Hip-width stance, grip outside knees, drive through floor maintaining neutral spine.
clinical_notes: Teach hip hinge pattern first. Progressive loading for LBP.

name: Good Morning
description: Barbell hip hinge for erector spinae and hamstrings.
instructions: Bar on upper back, hinge at hips pushing hips back. Return to upright.
clinical_notes: Excellent erector spinae loading. Start light.

name: Back Extension (45°)
description: Prone hip/spine extension on 45° bench.
instructions: On 45° back extension bench, lower torso, extend back to neutral.
clinical_notes: Do not hyperextend. Return to neutral only.

name: Superman
description: Prone extension exercise for posterior chain.
instructions: Prone, simultaneously lift arms and legs off floor. Hold briefly.
clinical_notes: Avoid with acute LBP. Moderate extension loading.

name: Shrug (Barbell)
description: Upper trapezius strengthening.
instructions: Hold barbell at thighs, shrug shoulders up toward ears. Hold at top.
clinical_notes: Can aggravate cervicogenic issues. Use cautiously.

name: Dip (Parallel Bar)
description: Bodyweight pushing for chest and triceps.
instructions: Support on parallel bars, lower body until upper arms parallel. Press up.
clinical_notes: Avoid past 90° if shoulder issues. Lean forward for chest emphasis.

name: Arnold Press
description: Rotational shoulder press for full deltoid activation.
instructions: Start with palms facing you, rotate and press overhead in one motion.
clinical_notes: Named after Arnold Schwarzenegger. Full ROM shoulder press.

name: Upright Row
description: Shoulder elevation exercise for deltoids and traps.
instructions: Narrow grip barbell, pull to chin height leading with elbows.
clinical_notes: HIGH IMPINGEMENT RISK. Use wide grip or substitute with lateral raises.

name: Cable External Rotation
description: Infraspinatus and teres minor strengthening.
instructions: Elbow at 90° at side, rotate forearm outward against cable. Control return.
clinical_notes: Fundamental rotator cuff exercise. Progress to 90° abduction.

name: Cable Internal Rotation
description: Subscapularis strengthening with cable.
instructions: Elbow at 90° at side, rotate forearm inward against cable.
clinical_notes: Balance with external rotation exercises. IR:ER ratio 3:2.

name: Prone Y Raise
description: Lower trapezius activation in prone.
instructions: Prone on bench, raise arms in Y pattern (120° from body). Thumbs up.
clinical_notes: Best lower trap activation exercise. Essential for scapular rehab.

name: Prone W Raise
description: Middle/lower trapezius and rotator cuff activation.
instructions: Prone on bench, raise arms in W pattern with external rotation.
clinical_notes: Combines retraction with external rotation. Excellent shoulder prehab.

name: Cat-Cow Mobilisation
description: Spinal flexion/extension mobility drill.
instructions: On all fours, alternate between arching (cow) and rounding (cat) spine.
clinical_notes: Gentle spinal mobilisation. Safe for most LBP patients.

name: Thoracic Rotation (Seated)
description: Seated thoracic spine rotation mobility.
instructions: Seated, arms crossed, rotate torso to each side. Keep hips stable.
clinical_notes: Essential for throwing athletes and office workers.

name: Pec Stretch (Doorway)
description: Pectoralis major and minor stretch.
instructions: Forearm on doorframe at 90°, step through doorway to stretch chest.
clinical_notes: Critical for upper crossed syndrome. Vary arm height for fiber targeting.

name: Upper Trapezius Stretch
description: Lateral neck flexion stretch for upper trap.
instructions: Tilt head to one side, gently pull with hand. Keep shoulders down.
clinical_notes: Common tight muscle in office workers. Gentle pressure only.

name: Levator Scapulae Stretch
description: Stretch for levator scapulae muscle.
instructions: Look down toward armpit, gently pull head with hand. Feel stretch at back of neck.
clinical_notes: Key muscle in cervicogenic headaches and neck pain.

name: Child's Pose
description: Restful spine flexion and lat stretch.
instructions: Kneel, sit back on heels, reach arms forward on floor.
clinical_notes: Excellent rest position. Modify with pillows if needed.

name: Prone Press-Up (McKenzie)
description: Lumbar extension for disc-related LBP.
instructions: Prone, press up through hands extending lumbar spine. Keep hips on floor.
clinical_notes: McKenzie directional preference exercise. For discogenic LBP with peripheralisation relief.

name: Hip Flexor Stretch (Thomas Position)
description: Supine hip flexor stretch targeting iliopsoas and rectus femoris.
instructions: Lie at edge of table, pull one knee to chest, let other leg hang. Feel stretch in front of hip.
clinical_notes: Also used as Thomas Test for hip flexor tightness assessment.

name: Adductor Stretch (Side Lunge)
description: Standing adductor and groin stretch.
instructions: Wide stance, shift weight to one side bending that knee. Feel inner thigh stretch.
clinical_notes: For groin tightness and adductor flexibility.

name: Lat Stretch (Overhead)
description: Standing or kneeling latissimus dorsi stretch.
instructions: Grab overhead bar or doorframe, lean away stretching lat and side body.
clinical_notes: Improves shoulder flexion ROM. Good for swimmers and climbers.

name: Open-Chain Flexor Digitorum Superficialis (Right) Strengthening
description: Isolated open-chain loading of Flexor Digitorum Superficialis (Right).
instructions: Distal limb free; resist movement with band/cuff weight. Move only the segment driven by Flexor Digitorum Superficialis (Right).
clinical_notes: Useful for early post-op when closed-chain loading is restricted.

name: Flexor Digitorum Superficialis (Right) Eccentric Loading
description: Eccentric strengthening protocol for Flexor Digitorum Superficialis (Right) tendon.
instructions: Load Flexor Digitorum Superficialis (Right) concentrically with assistance from contralateral side, then control 3-second eccentric phase.
clinical_notes: Evidence-based for chronic tendinopathy. Mild discomfort (3-4/10) acceptable; sharp pain is not.

name: Plyometric Drill for Adductor Pollicis (Oblique head) (Left)
description: Stretch-shortening cycle drill loading Adductor Pollicis (Oblique head) (Left).
instructions: Perform jump/bound focusing on minimal ground contact time. Land softly with full Adductor Pollicis (Oblique head) (Left) engagement.
clinical_notes: Late-stage rehab only. Ensure adequate strength base (≥85% LSI) before initiation.

name: Adductor Pollicis (Oblique head) (Left) Dynamic Stretch
description: Active dynamic mobility for the Adductor Pollicis (Oblique head) (Left).
instructions: Move the joint actively through full ROM of the Adductor Pollicis (Oblique head) (Left), holding end range briefly before returning.
clinical_notes: Use as warm-up. Avoid bouncing; controlled motion only.

name: Open-Chain Splenius Cervicis Strengthening
description: Isolated open-chain loading of Splenius Cervicis.
instructions: Distal limb free; resist movement with band/cuff weight. Move only the segment driven by Splenius Cervicis.
clinical_notes: Useful for early post-op when closed-chain loading is restricted.

name: PNF Stretch for Splenius Cervicis
description: Contract-relax PNF technique for the Splenius Cervicis.
instructions: Passively stretch Splenius Cervicis to first resistance. Contract isometrically 5 sec, relax, then increase stretch.
clinical_notes: Greater ROM gains than static stretching for most muscle groups (Sharman 2006).

name: Foam Rolling – Extensor Carpi Radialis Longus (Right)
description: Self-myofascial release for Extensor Carpi Radialis Longus (Right).
instructions: Place foam roller under Extensor Carpi Radialis Longus (Right). Roll slowly from origin to insertion, pausing on tender points 30 sec.
clinical_notes: Improves short-term ROM without strength loss; less effective than mobility drills for long-term ROM.

name: Heavy Slow Resistance for Extensor Carpi Radialis Longus (Right)
description: HSR protocol targeting Extensor Carpi Radialis Longus (Right)-related tendon.
instructions: Use 6-8RM load. 3-second concentric, 3-second eccentric. Tempo controlled, full ROM.
clinical_notes: HSR has equivalent outcomes to eccentric-only protocols and superior compliance (Beyer 2015).

name: Plyometric Drill for External Intercostal (4th space)
description: Stretch-shortening cycle drill loading External Intercostal (4th space).
instructions: Perform jump/bound focusing on minimal ground contact time. Land softly with full External Intercostal (4th space) engagement.
clinical_notes: Late-stage rehab only. Ensure adequate strength base (≥85% LSI) before initiation.

name: External Intercostal (4th space) Eccentric Loading
description: Eccentric strengthening protocol for External Intercostal (4th space) tendon.
instructions: Load External Intercostal (4th space) concentrically with assistance from contralateral side, then control 3-second eccentric phase.
clinical_notes: Evidence-based for chronic tendinopathy. Mild discomfort (3-4/10) acceptable; sharp pain is not.

name: Heavy Slow Resistance for External Intercostal (2nd space)
description: HSR protocol targeting External Intercostal (2nd space)-related tendon.
instructions: Use 6-8RM load. 3-second concentric, 3-second eccentric. Tempo controlled, full ROM.
clinical_notes: HSR has equivalent outcomes to eccentric-only protocols and superior compliance (Beyer 2015).

name: PNF Stretch for External Intercostal (2nd space)
description: Contract-relax PNF technique for the External Intercostal (2nd space).
instructions: Passively stretch External Intercostal (2nd space) to first resistance. Contract isometrically 5 sec, relax, then increase stretch.
clinical_notes: Greater ROM gains than static stretching for most muscle groups (Sharman 2006).

name: Adductor Magnus (Hamstring Part) (Left) Eccentric Loading
description: Eccentric strengthening protocol for Adductor Magnus (Hamstring Part) (Left) tendon.
instructions: Load Adductor Magnus (Hamstring Part) (Left) concentrically with assistance from contralateral side, then control 3-second eccentric phase.
clinical_notes: Evidence-based for chronic tendinopathy. Mild discomfort (3-4/10) acceptable; sharp pain is not.

name: Adductor Magnus (Hamstring Part) (Left) Isometric Hold
description: Isometric strengthening of the Adductor Magnus (Hamstring Part) (Left) at mid-range.
instructions: Position joint at mid-range. Contract Adductor Magnus (Hamstring Part) (Left) against immovable resistance. Breathe normally throughout the hold.
clinical_notes: Useful early-stage rehab when dynamic loading provokes pain (e.g., tendinopathy 0/10 reactive phase).

name: PNF Stretch for Flexor Digiti Minimi Brevis (Hand) (Right)
description: Contract-relax PNF technique for the Flexor Digiti Minimi Brevis (Hand) (Right).
instructions: Passively stretch Flexor Digiti Minimi Brevis (Hand) (Right) to first resistance. Contract isometrically 5 sec, relax, then increase stretch.
clinical_notes: Greater ROM gains than static stretching for most muscle groups (Sharman 2006).

name: Closed-Chain Flexor Digiti Minimi Brevis (Hand) (Right) Activation
description: Functional closed-chain activation of Flexor Digiti Minimi Brevis (Hand) (Right).
instructions: Use bodyweight in standing/weight-bearing position. Cue Flexor Digiti Minimi Brevis (Hand) (Right) engagement during functional task.
clinical_notes: Improves transfer of strength to ADLs. Combine with proprioceptive challenge for return-to-sport.

name: Closed-Chain Intertransversarii Lumborum (Mediales) Activation

name: Plyometric Drill for 2nd Palmar Interosseous (Right)

instructions: Distal limb free; resist movement with band/cuff weight. Move only the segment driven by Fibularis Tertius (Peroneus Tertius).
clinical_notes: Useful for early post-op when closed-chain loading is restricted.

name: Proprioceptive Training – Supinator (Right)
description: Neuromuscular control drill emphasising Supinator (Right).
instructions: Perform single-limb stance with eyes closed/foam pad. Cue postural alignment via Supinator (Right) activation.
clinical_notes: Reduces re-injury rate after ankle sprain & ACL injury (Hubscher 2010 meta-analysis).

name: Open-Chain Supinator (Right) Strengthening
description: Isolated open-chain loading of Supinator (Right).
instructions: Distal limb free; resist movement with band/cuff weight. Move only the segment driven by Supinator (Right).
clinical_notes: Useful for early post-op when closed-chain loading is restricted.

name: PNF Stretch for External Intercostals
description: Contract-relax PNF technique for the External Intercostals.
instructions: Passively stretch External Intercostals to first resistance. Contract isometrically 5 sec, relax, then increase stretch.
clinical_notes: Greater ROM gains than static stretching for most muscle groups (Sharman 2006).

name: Heavy Slow Resistance for External Intercostals
description: HSR protocol targeting External Intercostals-related tendon.
instructions: Use 6-8RM load. 3-second concentric, 3-second eccentric. Tempo controlled, full ROM.
clinical_notes: HSR has equivalent outcomes to eccentric-only protocols and superior compliance (Beyer 2015).

name: Plyometric Drill for 3rd Palmar Interosseous (Right)
description: Stretch-shortening cycle drill loading 3rd Palmar Interosseous (Right).
instructions: Perform jump/bound focusing on minimal ground contact time. Land softly with full 3rd Palmar Interosseous (Right) engagement.
clinical_notes: Late-stage rehab only. Ensure adequate strength base (≥85% LSI) before initiation.

name: Closed-Chain 3rd Palmar Interosseous (Right) Activation
description: Functional closed-chain activation of 3rd Palmar Interosseous (Right).
instructions: Use bodyweight in standing/weight-bearing position. Cue 3rd Palmar Interosseous (Right) engagement during functional task.
clinical_notes: Improves transfer of strength to ADLs. Combine with proprioceptive challenge for return-to-sport.

name: Open-Chain Internal Intercostals (Interchondral portion) Strengthening
description: Isolated open-chain loading of Internal Intercostals (Interchondral portion).
instructions: Distal limb free; resist movement with band/cuff weight. Move only the segment driven by Internal Intercostals (Interchondral portion).
clinical_notes: Useful for early post-op when closed-chain loading is restricted.

name: Internal Intercostals (Interchondral portion) Eccentric Loading
description: Eccentric strengthening protocol for Internal Intercostals (Interchondral portion) tendon.
instructions: Load Internal Intercostals (Interchondral portion) concentrically with assistance from contralateral side, then control 3-second eccentric phase.
clinical_notes: Evidence-based for chronic tendinopathy. Mild discomfort (3-4/10) acceptable; sharp pain is not.

name: Open-Chain External Intercostal (5th space) Strengthening
description: Isolated open-chain loading of External Intercostal (5th space).
instructions: Distal limb free; resist movement with band/cuff weight. Move only the segment driven by External Intercostal (5th space).
clinical_notes: Useful for early post-op when closed-chain loading is restricted.

name: Foam Rolling – External Intercostal (5th space)
description: Self-myofascial release for External Intercostal (5th space).
instructions: Place foam roller under External Intercostal (5th space). Roll slowly from origin to insertion, pausing on tender points 30 sec.
clinical_notes: Improves short-term ROM without strength loss; less effective than mobility drills for long-term ROM.
